addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: If the Chrome browser tools are deferred (must be loaded via ToolSearch before use), load them with ToolSearch before calling them, and batch every tool you expect to need into ONE ToolSearch call (the select query accepts a comma-separated list). Do NOT load tools one at a time; each separate ToolSearch call wastes a full round-trip.**

Start a browser task whose tools are not yet loaded with a single call loading the core set:

ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp,mcp__claude-in-chrome__navigate,mcp__claude-in-chrome__computer,mcp__claude-in-chrome__read_page,mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_create_mcp"

Add task-specific tools to the same call when the task obviously needs them: read_console_messages / read_network_requests for debugging, form_input for forms, gif_creator for recordings, javascript_tool for page scripting. Only issue a second ToolSearch if the task later needs a tool you did not anticipate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact page can be granted — behavior static HTML cannot provide on its own, such as the page reading live or connected data, keeping state shared across viewers, or updating and republishing itself. Serves this user's live capability roster and the typed call definitions. Load it whenever the user asks for an artifact needing any such runtime behavior.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

_No cycle currently in progress._

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
   sequence:
   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
     stages, ochids injected both directions)
   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
   - publish

   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
     normally, before the rebase.
   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
     operation).
   - Interim recipe only: the refactor program's
     [trapezoid close-out stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out)
     is the end state; this pause point remains the manual
     path until it lands.

2. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md))
   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
   former Todos live in
   [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md).
   - Stages in execution order: DRY facade → hygiene
     riders → facade owns topology → de-gitify init →
     split push.rs → jj-lib migration → push body-intro
     validation → trapezoid close-out → por → dual
     conversion.
3. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
   the cycle after 0.65.2.
   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
     not a pre-assigned version?
     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
       A contributors email address would do it, but also
       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.
4. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
5. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch

aiTitle: Implement non-ff merge and trapezoidal close-out

content.content:
120	   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
121	   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
122	   single-mode sync cycle.
123	   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
124	     halts the push before anything is committed); the
125	     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
126	   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
127	     nothing invokes it.
128	   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to
129	     push's commit-stage rollback (today it auto-runs
130	     `jj op restore`, hiding the evidence).
131	6. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
132	   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
133	   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
134	   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
135	   `TODO.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
136	   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
137	   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
138	   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,
139	     `fix-todo` → `fix-numbering` — they validate numbered-
140	     sequence integrity, not todos specifically.
141	   - Generic detection: for every `#…#` section, validate the
142	     column-0 `^\d+\.␠` entries form a contiguous 1..N run.
143	     Drops the Todo/Bugs special-casing; auto-covers
144	     `## Ideas` and any new numbered section. Keep the
145	     column-0 anchor so indented sub-lists aren't counted.
146	   - Default scope: a fixed list of sequence-managed notes
147	     files (`TODO.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`) so the
148	     no-arg pre-commit run covers them all. Fixed rather than
149	     a `notes/**.md` walk because prose docs
150	     (`cycle-protocol.md`, design notes) carry ordinary
151	     numbered lists that aren't managed sequences — a walk
152	     would false-positive (markdown renders `1. 1. 1.` as
153	     1-2-3, a legitimate prose pattern).
154	   - Override args follow the `--init-from` convention:
155	     positional files/dirs (a dir → its `*.md`) plus an
156	     `@<file>` manifest, additive — for ad-hoc validation of
157	     a specific file.
158	   - Add wrapper-level tests while restructuring: the analyze
159	     cores are covered (`todo_helpers` 15 tests,
160	     `desc_helpers` 22) but the `validate-todo` / `fix-todo` /
161	     `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` wrappers have none — file
162	     I/O, output formatting, exit codes, and the no-arg
163	     default path (changed to `TODO.md` at 0.69.2-2) are
164	     unexercised.
165	   - Open: revisit fixed-vs-glob at implementation if the
166	     fixed list proves annoying to maintain.
167	7. **pre-commit: single rule (no docs skip) + doc validators.**
168	   The pre-commit (cargo cycle: fmt/clippy/test/install) only
169	   checks code, so it's "skip-able for purely-docs commits" —
170	   but that exception is exactly where checks slip (skipped on
171	   0.62.0-7/-8 until caught). (Since 0.69.0-3 push's
172	   `preflight` no longer re-runs the cargo cycle — vc-x1
173	   assumes nothing about repo contents — so the pre-commit is
174	   the *only* gate, strengthening the no-skip case.)
175	   - Adopt one rule, no exception: the pre-commit runs before
176	     Work review on every commit. (docs: AGENTS.md Cycle
177	     Protocol summary + cycle-protocol.md per-commit-flow.)
178	   - Enrich the pre-commit so it's meaningful on docs commits:
179	     add the doc validators — `validate-numbering` (its own
180	     Todo, a prereq) plus `validate-repo` when it exists.
181	     Whether push's `preflight` may run them needs a decision
182	     against the content-agnostic principle (they read
183	     `notes/` — repo content; the repo-declared-checks idea
184	     was rejected 2026-07-15 in favor of "run checks
185	     yourself").
186	   - This dissolves the docs exception: with doc validators in
187	     the pre-commit there's always something to validate, so
188	     the carve-out stops making sense.
189	   - Its own near-term cycle (chosen over a 0.61.1 insert to
190	     avoid rewriting published 0.62.0-x history); no version
191	     pre-assigned — see the Todo "Version-number protocol is
192	     fragile" on fragile version targets.
193	8. **vc-x1 push: record uncovered code commits (N:1 code↔bot).**
194	   Today push assumes 1:1 symmetric WC commits with shared
195	   title/body. The interop / adoption scenario breaks that:
196	   the code side is worked single-repo style (commit +
197	   `jj git push` / `git push`, no `vc-x1 push` in the loop),
198	   so no bot pairings exist — one bot commit then records
199	   every code commit not yet covered by a prior `ochid:`,
200	   via a multi-line `ochid:` per the design in [[10]].
201	   - Also covers a cycle held local and published all at
202	     once (the ochid-trailers section's "one ochid per Work
203	     commit" on merge close-out) — work commits never
204	     individually paired.
205	   - Out of scope: the trapezoid close-out. That flow stays
206	     1:1 (the close-out pair commits normally, then the
207	     merge rebase; chids survive rebase, so ochids stay
208	     valid); its enabler is the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause
209	     point between commit and publish stages".
210	   - Teach push to:
211	     - detect the shape (code WC empty, uncovered commits at
212	       the bookmark)
213	     - skip `commit-app`
214	     - compose a `.claude`-specific message
215	     - emit one `ochid:` line per uncovered commit
216	   - Open: computing "uncovered" — likely a revset from the
217	     code bookmark back to the newest commit referenced by
218	     the bot journal's ochids.
219	9. **Run validate-bot at every vc-x1 invocation
220	   (config-gated).** The check is one jj spawn
221	   (`jj bookmark list main --all-remotes`), cheap enough
222	   to run at every execution — noted 2026-07-15 as a
223	   "could, not should". Design points:
224	   - locate the bot repo (`<cwd>/.claude` or config;
225	     shares the lookup with the refactor program's
226	     [facade-owns-topology stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-facade-owns-topology))
227	     and silently skip when absent
228	   - severity knob in `.vc-config.toml`
229	     (`warn|error|off`): unrelated commands (fix-todo)
230	     warn at most; push / squash-push / validate-bot
231	     already have their own handling from 0.69.0-3
232	10. **README: audit flag tables and examples against the
233	    current CLI.** 0.69.0-4 fixed the init section (it
234	    documented retired `--owner` / `--dir` / `--repo-local`
235	    flags) and the 0.69.0 surfaces, but the README's other
236	    tables (clone, symlink, sync, revert, list/desc/chid/
237	    show) have never had a systematic `-h` comparison and
238	    drift silently.
239	    - Sweep each section against `vc-x1 <cmd> -h`.
240	    - Consider regenerating transcripts via support
241	      scripts (the gen-exmpl pattern) so examples stay
242	      reproducible.
243	11. **Shared-doc sync: As-built ladder rungs carry `[[N]]`
244	    commit refs.** Adopted in chores-13 (0.69.2 ladder,
245	    backfilled during 0.70.0-0): each rung is prepended
246	    with its commit reference so the rung↔commit
247	    correlation is direct; `Commits:` stays as the
248	    section-level list. The convention's home —
249	    cycle-protocol.md Close-out ("Add an `### As-built
250	    ladder`…") — is in the byte-identical shared doc set
251	    (vc-x1, vc-template-x1, iiac-perf), so the doc edit
252	    needs a coordinated three-project sync, not a
253	    mid-cycle local change.
254	12. **Shared-doc sync: per-commit chores convention.**
255	    0.71.0 changed how chores are recorded — each work commit
256	    appends its As-built rung + narrative as it lands, rather
257	    than the narrative waiting for close-out. That wording edit
258	    was made locally in vc-x1's `cycle-protocol.md` / `AGENTS.md`
259	    (the byte-identical shared doc set), so vc-template-x1 and
260	    iiac-perf now diverge until the same edit is applied there —
261	    a coordinated three-project sync (same family as Todo #11).
262	13. **config: extract flag-backed key descriptions from Clap.**
263	    `config`'s key descriptions live in `config_schema.rs`
264	    (`doc`/`used_by`). For the handful of keys that map 1:1 to a
265	    CLI flag (`bot-session.col-width` ↔ `--col-width`,
266	    `--result-lines`), the description could instead be pulled
267	    from the Clap arg's help via `Cli::command()` introspection,
268	    so `vc-x1 config` and `--help` share one source and can't
269	    disagree.
270	    - Only ~2 keys map cleanly (most are config-only, flag-sets,
271	      or value-providers), so it's a partial source — the schema
272	      stays authoritative for the rest.
273	    - Defaults still come from the schema/consts (the args
274	      dropped `default_value_t`, so Clap no longer holds them).
275	    - Output format is unchanged, only the text source — no
276	      rework of the 0.71.0-9 rendering.
277	
278	## Ideas

6. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
   `TODO.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,
     `fix-todo` → `fix-numbering` — they validate numbered-
     sequence integrity, not todos specifically.
   - Generic detection: for every `#…#` section, validate the
     column-0 `^\d+\.␠` entries form a contiguous 1..N run.
     Drops the Todo/Bugs special-casing; auto-covers
     `## Ideas` and any new numbered section. Keep the
     column-0 anchor so indented sub-lists aren't counted.
   - Default scope: a fixed list of sequence-managed notes
     files (`TODO.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`) so the
     no-arg pre-commit run covers them all. Fixed rather than
     a `notes/**.md` walk because prose docs
     (`cycle-protocol.md`, design notes) carry ordinary
     numbered lists that aren't managed sequences — a walk
     would false-positive (markdown renders `1. 1. 1.` as
     1-2-3, a legitimate prose pattern).
   - Override args follow the `--init-from` convention:
     positional files/dirs (a dir → its `*.md`) plus an
     `@<file>` manifest, additive — for ad-hoc validation of
     a specific file.
   - Add wrapper-level tests while restructuring: the analyze
     cores are covered (`todo_helpers` 15 tests,
     `desc_helpers` 22) but the `validate-todo` / `fix-todo` /
     `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` wrappers have none — file
     I/O, output formatting, exit codes, and the no-arg
     default path (changed to `TODO.md` at 0.69.2-2) are
     unexercised.
   - Open: revisit fixed-vs-glob at implementation if the
     fixed list proves annoying to maintain.
7. **pre-commit: single rule (no docs skip) + doc validators.**

   checks code, so it's "skip-able for purely-docs commits" —
   but that exception is exactly where checks slip (skipped on
   0.62.0-7/-8 until caught). (Since 0.69.0-3 push's
   `preflight` no longer re-runs the cargo cycle — vc-x1
   assumes nothing about repo contents — so the pre-commit is
   the *only* gate, strengthening the no-skip case.)

     fragile" on fragile version targets.

   title/body. The interop / adoption scenario breaks that:
   the code side is worked single-repo style (commit +

   so no bot pairings exist — one bot commit then records
   every code commit not yet covered by a prior `ochid:`,
   via a multi-line `ochid:` per the design in [[10]].
   - Also covers a cycle held local and published all at
     once (the ochid-trailers section's "one ochid per Work
     commit" on merge close-out) — work commits never
     individually paired.
   - Out of scope: the trapezoid close-out. That flow stays
     1:1 (the close-out pair commits normally, then the
     merge rebase; chids survive rebase, so ochids stay
     valid); its enabler is the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause
     point between commit and publish stages".
   - Teach push to:
     - detect the shape (code WC empty, uncovered commits at
       the bookmark)
     - skip `commit-app`
     - compose a `.claude`-specific message
     - emit one `ochid:` line per uncovered commit
   - Open: computing "uncovered" — likely a revset from the
     code bookmark back to the newest commit referenced by
     the bot journal's ochids.
9. **Run validate-bot at every vc-x1 invocation
   (config-gated).** The check is one jj spawn
   (`jj bookmark list main --all-remotes`), cheap enough
   to run at every execution — noted 2026-07-15 as a
   "could, not should". Design points:
   - locate the bot repo (`<cwd>/.claude` or config;
     shares the lookup with the refactor program's

     and silently skip when absent
   - severity knob in `.vc-config.toml`
     (`warn|error|off`): unrelated commands (fix-todo)

10. **README: audit flag tables and examples against the
    current CLI.** 0.69.0-4 fixed the init section (it
    documented retired `--owner` / `--dir` / `--repo-local`

278	## Ideas
279	
280	 Items not yet solid enough for `## Todo` (or surfaced
281	 during close-out / end-of-day before they're fully
282	 formed). Triaged at the next Preparation: promote to
283	 `## Todo` / `notes/todo-backlog.md`, fold into a
284	 picked-up cycle, or drop.
285	
286	1. **Codify ochid invariant + bot-repo rules + squash
287	   gating + cross-repo migration in
288	   `notes/cycle-protocol.md`.** Was planned for
289	   `0.59.0-2` but deferred when 0.59.0 closed out via
290	   squash + Option F (manual bot-side rewrite). The
291	   rules were exercised manually for the close-out;
292	   this Idea formally codifies them.
293	   - Codify the **ochid invariant** in `## ochid
294	     trailers`: every public ochid must resolve in the
295	     public graph.
296	   - Codify bot-repo rules: never squashed; descriptions
297	     editable (`jj describe` preserves chid).
298	   - Codify squash gating: until `vc-x1 push --squash`
299	     exists, manual symmetric squash (Option F: app
300	     squash + bot-side trailer rewrite + force-push) is
301	     the standard recipe; merge non-ff is the default
302	     shape for multi-commit cycles (the concrete recipe is
303	     its own Todo — "merge-non-ff recipe").
304	   - Sketch cross-repo migration: ochids change at
305	     every merge until the change reaches the canonical
306	     repo's `main`.
307	   - Apply Ideas-aware Preparation/Close-out updates:
308	     Preparation triages `## Ideas` (promote / fold /
309	     drop) before declaring the plan; Close-out
310	     captures unresolved follow-ups into `## Ideas`.
311	
312	2. **`vc` as a code+conversation provenance tool (grander
313	   ambition).** Today `vc-x1` manages a dual repo (code +
314	   `.claude`) cross-linked by `ochid:`. The larger aim is
315	   to *surface* that link — view history with the
316	   conversation and the code side by side: provenance, the
317	   *why* of a change, not just the *what*. The dual-repo +
318	   `ochid` design is already the substrate; the cross-links
319	   make code↔conversation navigable, so the viewer is UI
320	   over an already-solved data link.
321	   - Build direction: keep resolution/assembly in `vc` — an
322	     editor-agnostic Rust engine/lib extending the
323	     `show` / `chid` / `desc` family ("given a commit,
324	     resolve its ochid and assemble the paired diff +
325	     conversation slice"); the editor add-on is a thin
326	     presentation layer over it.
327	   - Front-end leans a Zed add-on (Rust, preferred), maybe
328	     VSCode / other. Verify Zed's extension API can host a
329	     rich side-by-side panel before committing — an
330	     editor-agnostic core hedges the bet.
331	   - `vc-x2`? A rewrite is unwarranted: the audit's
332	     Commonality pass found the architecture sound (por is
333	     bolted on where an existing good pattern wasn't
334	     applied) — equalize incrementally. "vc-x2" only makes
335	     sense if the viewer changes the *core* architecture
336	     (an index / daemon / data model). Separate
337	     engine-rewrite (no) from product-reposition (open).
338	   - Possible artifact: a top-level
339	     `notes/design-cli/vision.md` framing the direction,
340	     with the parity and conversion docs as sub-designs.
341	3. **Restructure the design-cli parity docs (target
342	   0.63.0).** `por-dual-parity-audit.md` (~1200 lines)
343	   fuses a *frozen* audit (the `## 1`–`## 8` snapshot
344	   evidence) with a *living* design (axes, decisions,
345	   matrix, gap list); the "audit" name undersells it and
346	   the halves have different lifecycles. And
347	   `por-dual-parity.md` (the stub) overlaps on parity but
348	   uniquely holds the `por ↔ dual` conversion design.
349	   - Split the audit doc into a frozen audit snapshot + a
350	     living design doc (names TBD; could reclaim
351	     `por-dual-parity.md` for the design).
352	   - Refocus the stub to conversion-only and rename (e.g.
353	     `por-dual-conversion.md`); drop its redundant parity
354	     half.
355	   - Repoint refs (`todo.md` `[1]` + the `por → dual` Todo,
356	     `copying.md`, the audit's internal anchors + Reading
357	     guide) and validate; `chores-10/11/12` mentions are
358	     historical and stay.
359	   - Promote the Gap-list items to anchored
360	     `#### Gap N — <title>` sub-headings so cross-cycle
361	     citations can deep-link a specific gap (markdown
362	     anchors headings, not list items). Trade-off: stable
363	     anchors, but the ordinal lives in the heading text
364	     (manual renumber on reorder) — fine for a consumed
365	     backlog. The 3 `Gap #N` links in the `0.62.0`
366	     close-out chores narrative resolve only to the section
367	     until this lands.
368	   - Deferred from the 0.62.0 close-out: close-out is
369	     bookkeeping-only, and the split is substantive,
370	     anchor-heavy work warranting its own cycle.

   - Promote the Gap-list items to anchored

  code-declared config schema registry (`config_schema.rs`) as
  the single source of truth for every settable config key; the
  new `config` command (print, `--home`, `--validate`), init's
  commented `.vc-config.toml` defaults, and bot-session's
  `--result-lines`/`--col-width` config layer all derive from it,
  so they can't drift. Also a `notes/transcript-format.md` SSOT +
  sample for the bot-session format, and a sweep retiring the
  ambiguous "dotted" wording [[7]]
- bot-session: --fields / --unknown output clarification — the
  inventory views are now documented rather than opaque:
  [transcript-format.md](notes/transcript-format.md) defines
  entry / entry type and the `.`/`[]` field notation with a
  bot-session example (0.71.0-8), and the ambiguous "dotted"
  wording was retired (0.71.0-7). In-view column labeling

1	# Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process (2026-07-16)
2	
3	The plan-and-design document for the jj refactor program.
4	Version-control operations are scattered hand-rolled
5	`run("jj", …)` spawns (~30 query sites plus every mutation)
6	with per-module private wrappers, plus raw-git vestiges in
7	init. Spawning means stderr parsing instead of typed errors,
8	and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside
9	our control — the push `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]] can't
10	be retried where it fails.
11	
12	How this file works:
13	
14	- Each stage below is a `##` section in execution order;
15	  each is roughly one `X.Y.Z` cycle. Section anchors are the
16	  durable way to reference a stage from TODO.md, chores, or
17	  bugs.md.
18	- This file owns the forward plan and design. When a stage
19	  ships, its `chores-NN.md` section records the commits and
20	  narrative as usual; the stage section here gets a one-line
21	  status link to that chores section — no duplication.
22	- The `## Todo` entry "Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib
23	  in-process; end subprocess spawning" in
24	  [TODO.md](../TODO.md) is the short pointer here.
25	
26	Absorbed former Todos: "Dedup jj subprocess queries behind a
27	typed facade", "Split push.rs state machinery", "vc-x1 push:
28	validate body opens with an intro paragraph", "Make the bot
29	repo directory name configurable", "Terminology stragglers
30	from the work/bot sweep", "single-field `options_flags`
31	leaves → `value` field", "`por → dual` conversion",
32	"`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization".
33	
34	## Stage: DRY facade
35	
36	~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
37	"--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
38	module has quietly grown a private wrapper. Spotted at
39	0.69.0-2: `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind" but is
40	the Nth reinvention.
41	
42	- One facade module (name open: `src/jj.rs` or a `common`
43	  split): `jj_log(repo, rev, template)` plus typed helpers —
44	  `rev_exists`, `chid_of`, `cid_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty`.
45	- Existing reinventions to fold:
46	  `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
47	  rev_is_empty_undescribed}`, `push::jj_log_empty` + four
48	  inline `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
49	  three template variants in `sync.rs`.
50	- Fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw `std::process::Command` +
51	  its own `@origin:` prefix parse) onto
52	  `common::verify_tracking`'s parser — two tracking parsers
53	  will eventually disagree.
54	- Unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
55	  `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs `common::extract_ochid`
56	  (jj-lib `Commit`-based); one string-level parser in
57	  `desc_helpers` both can call.
58	- Test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` / `cid()` /
59	  `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
60	  `push/integration_tests.rs`, `sync/integration_tests.rs`,
61	  and `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`.
62	
63	## Stage: hygiene riders
64	
65	Sweeps that churn the same lines the facade stage rewrites;
66	done early so later stages diff cleanly.
67	
68	- Terminology stragglers from the 0.69.0-4 work/bot sweep:
69	  - `-s` scope keyword `code` (renaming to `work` is a
70	    breaking CLI change; `bot` already matches)
71	  - init's literal remote names `remote-code.git` /
72	    `remote-claude.git`
73	  - identifiers `derive_session_url`, `claude_path`,
74	    `Fixture.claude`, `Side::Code`
75	  - decide rename vs document-as-historical (overlaps
76	    [Stage: facade owns topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology))
77	- Single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field (the
78	  0.47.0 convention from the `squash` leaf): sweep `repo`,
79	  `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`
80	  + consumers (`init.rs`, tests). Open: could a single field
81	  be a type/enum instead of a struct, eliminating the
82	  `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` naming issue entirely.
83	
84	## Stage: facade owns topology
85	
86	Repo resolution becomes facade state, not per-command
87	preludes.
88	
89	- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization: replace the
90	  `other_repo_from_config` prelude in both
91	  (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`) with
92	  scope-aware resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot` when
93	  absent; body unchanged. The 0.61.0 audit [[2]] called this
94	  the cheapest equalization and the prototype for the
95	  topology-from-config rule (topology from `default_scope`,
96	  not a flag); the remaining implementation gaps live in
97	  [[2]]'s `## Gap list`.
98	- Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
99	  hardcoded `.claude`; decided 2026-07-15 the name need not
100	  be `.claude`). The literal is embedded across
101	  init/clone/push/sync, `claude_path()`, ochid path
102	  semantics (`/.claude/<chid>`), the
103	  `~/.claude/projects/<path>` symlink, and docs;
104	  `.vc-config.toml` already records workspace paths — the
105	  natural home. Open: migration of existing workspaces, the
106	  ochid prefix recorded in immutable history, the
107	  harness-controlled symlink location.
108	
109	## Stage: de-gitify init
110	
111	`init.rs::push_repo`'s strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate
112	flow is the step shape the abandoned submodule design
113	required [[3]]; the symlink design doesn't need jj evicted.
114	
115	- jj-only replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
116	  scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates the
117	  git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
118	  `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
119	  `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set main
120	  -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`.
121	- No `--allow-new` needed; push established tracking as a
122	  side effect (matches the test-fixture observation in
123	  [[4]]).
124	- Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
125	  `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.
126	
127	## Stage: split push.rs
128	
129	`push.rs` (~1.6k lines, the largest file) holds the `Stage`
130	machine, TOML state persistence, eight stage bodies, two
131	sanity verifiers, and the interactive gates; `sync/state.rs`
132	is the in-repo precedent.
133	
134	- Extract `push/state.rs` (`Stage`, `PushState`, state
135	  layout resolution) so the
136	  [jj-lib migration](#stage-jj-lib-migration) rewrites stage
137	  bodies in a file that reviews cleanly.
138	
139	## Stage: jj-lib migration
140	
141	Migrate the facade internals to jj-lib (already a dependency
142	for read-only commands); removes jj and git spawning
143	entirely.
144	
145	- Reads first: `jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors.
146	- Then mutations: commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
147	  fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store lift.
148	- The index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
149	  retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for the
150	  bugs.md `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]].
151	- `LocalBareInit`'s `git init --bare` becomes a gix call
152	  (jj-lib already pulls gix in).
153	
154	## Stage: push body-intro validation
155	
156	A bullet-first commit body trips jj's arg parser today
157	(`-m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a stray flag; hit on
158	0.62.0-5) and push fails with an opaque error.
159	
160	- Validate the body opens with a non-dash intro line (Prose
161	  Form) and report the offending first line with a clear,
162	  specific error — enforcing the intro is the intended
163	  behavior, not a parser fix.
164	- Workaround until then: prepend a non-dash intro sentence.
165	
166	## Stage: trapezoid close-out
167	
168	The close-out commit becomes a two-parent merge — first
169	parent the pre-cycle base (the parent of the ladder's first
170	commit), second parent the ladder tip (`-X`), the
171	`git merge --no-ff` convention — so
172	`git log --first-parent main` reads one commit per cycle
173	while the full ladder stays reachable via the second parent.
174	
175	- The merge itself is trivial in-process: a two-parent
176	  commit in one transaction; no conflicts possible since the
177	  base is an ancestor of the tip.
178	- The real work is ochid stamping — the bot commit carries
179	  one ochid per work commit in the cycle (the AGENTS.md
180	  merge non-ff shape) while push today stamps only the
181	  topmost commit.
182	- Teach `validate-desc` + the push sanity verifiers the new
183	  legal shape (two parents, N ochids).
184	- End state of the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause point between
185	  commit and publish stages", which stays as the interim
186	  manual recipe until this lands.
187	
188	## Stage: por → dual conversion
189	
190	Attach a bot companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an
191	existing por workspace; emit cross-links going forward.
192	Manual setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
193	proved arduous; should be a routine subcommand. Design stub
194	in [[5]] § 2.
195	
196	- Last on purpose: it leans on
197	  [facade topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology) and the
198	  in-process init pieces.
199	
200	## Access rule
201	
202	Today's implicit rule — read-only display commands use
203	jj-lib in-process, mutating commands shell out — dissolves
204	once mutations are in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the
205	end state instead.
206	
207	## Out of scope: still spawned
208	
209	- `gh` (remote provisioning) and `$EDITOR` stay spawned by
210	  design.
211	- The `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn goes away when the
212	  Todo "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process" lands —
213	  same direction, independently shippable, not part of this
214	  program.
215	- Beneficiary: the Todo "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
216	  invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
217	  in-process read.
218	
219	## Design risk: op-store coexistence
220	
221	We think in-process op-store writes must coexist with the
222	user's external `jj` CLI driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41
223	vs installed jj 0.40.0 format/version skew needs a
224	compatibility check before the
225	[mutation stage](#stage-jj-lib-migration) commits to the
226	approach.
227	
228	# References
229	
230	[1]: /notes/bugs.md
231	[2]: /notes/chores/chores-12.md#docs-pordual-parity-design-0610
232	[3]: /notes/chores/chores-03.md#submodule--ochid-circular-dependency-bug-0290
233	[4]: /notes/chores/chores-06.md#non-tracking-remote-bookmark-detection-design
234	[5]: /notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity.md

file.content:
# Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process (2026-07-16)

The plan-and-design document for the jj refactor program.
Version-control operations are scattered hand-rolled
`run("jj", …)` spawns (~30 query sites plus every mutation)
with per-module private wrappers, plus raw-git vestiges in
init. Spawning means stderr parsing instead of typed errors,
and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside
our control — the push `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]] can't
be retried where it fails.

How this file works:

- Each stage below is a `##` section in execution order;
  each is roughly one `X.Y.Z` cycle. Section anchors are the
  durable way to reference a stage from TODO.md, chores, or
  bugs.md.
- This file owns the forward plan and design. When a stage
  ships, its `chores-NN.md` section records the commits and
  narrative as usual; the stage section here gets a one-line
  status link to that chores section — no duplication.
- The `## Todo` entry "Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib
  in-process; end subprocess spawning" in
  [TODO.md](../TODO.md) is the short pointer here.

Absorbed former Todos: "Dedup jj subprocess queries behind a
typed facade", "Split push.rs state machinery", "vc-x1 push:
validate body opens with an intro paragraph", "Make the bot
repo directory name configurable", "Terminology stragglers
from the work/bot sweep", "single-field `options_flags`
leaves → `value` field", "`por → dual` conversion",
"`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization".

## Stage: DRY facade

~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
"--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
module has quietly grown a private wrapper. Spotted at
0.69.0-2: `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind" but is
the Nth reinvention.

- One facade module (name open: `src/jj.rs` or a `common`
  split): `jj_log(repo, rev, template)` plus typed helpers —
  `rev_exists`, `chid_of`, `cid_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty`.
- Existing reinventions to fold:
  `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
  rev_is_empty_undescribed}`, `push::jj_log_empty` + four
  inline `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
  three template variants in `sync.rs`.
- Fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw `std::process::Command` +
  its own `@origin:` prefix parse) onto
  `common::verify_tracking`'s parser — two tracking parsers
  will eventually disagree.
- Unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
  `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs `common::extract_ochid`
  (jj-lib `Commit`-based); one string-level parser in
  `desc_helpers` both can call.
- Test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` / `cid()` /
  `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
  `push/integration_tests.rs`, `sync/integration_tests.rs`,
  and `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`.

## Stage: hygiene riders

Sweeps that churn the same lines the facade stage rewrites;
done early so later stages diff cleanly.

- Terminology stragglers from the 0.69.0-4 work/bot sweep:
  - `-s` scope keyword `code` (renaming to `work` is a
    breaking CLI change; `bot` already matches)
  - init's literal remote names `remote-code.git` /
    `remote-claude.git`
  - identifiers `derive_session_url`, `claude_path`,
    `Fixture.claude`, `Side::Code`
  - decide rename vs document-as-historical (overlaps
    [Stage: facade owns topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology))
- Single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field (the
  0.47.0 convention from the `squash` leaf): sweep `repo`,
  `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`
  + consumers (`init.rs`, tests). Open: could a single field
  be a type/enum instead of a struct, eliminating the
  `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` naming issue entirely.

## Stage: facade owns topology

Repo resolution becomes facade state, not per-command
preludes.

- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization: replace the
  `other_repo_from_config` prelude in both
  (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`) with
  scope-aware resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot` when
  absent; body unchanged. The 0.61.0 audit [[2]] called this
  the cheapest equalization and the prototype for the
  topology-from-config rule (topology from `default_scope`,
  not a flag); the remaining implementation gaps live in
  [[2]]'s `## Gap list`.
- Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
  hardcoded `.claude`; decided 2026-07-15 the name need not
  be `.claude`). The literal is embedded across
  init/clone/push/sync, `claude_path()`, ochid path
  semantics (`/.claude/<chid>`), the
  `~/.claude/projects/<path>` symlink, and docs;
  `.vc-config.toml` already records workspace paths — the
  natural home. Open: migration of existing workspaces, the
  ochid prefix recorded in immutable history, the
  harness-controlled symlink location.

## Stage: de-gitify init

`init.rs::push_repo`'s strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate
flow is the step shape the abandoned submodule design
required [[3]]; the symlink design doesn't need jj evicted.

- jj-only replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
  scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates the
  git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
  `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
  `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set main
  -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`.
- No `--allow-new` needed; push established tracking as a
  side effect (matches the test-fixture observation in
  [[4]]).
- Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
  `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.

## Stage: split push.rs

`push.rs` (~1.6k lines, the largest file) holds the `Stage`
machine, TOML state persistence, eight stage bodies, two
sanity verifiers, and the interactive gates; `sync/state.rs`
is the in-repo precedent.

- Extract `push/state.rs` (`Stage`, `PushState`, state
  layout resolution) so the
  [jj-lib migration](#stage-jj-lib-migration) rewrites stage
  bodies in a file that reviews cleanly.

## Stage: jj-lib migration

Migrate the facade internals to jj-lib (already a dependency
for read-only commands); removes jj and git spawning
entirely.

- Reads first: `jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors.
- Then mutations: commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
  fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store lift.
- The index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
  retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for the
  bugs.md `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]].
- `LocalBareInit`'s `git init --bare` becomes a gix call
  (jj-lib already pulls gix in).

## Stage: push body-intro validation

A bullet-first commit body trips jj's arg parser today
(`-m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a stray flag; hit on
0.62.0-5) and push fails with an opaque error.

- Validate the body opens with a non-dash intro line (Prose
  Form) and report the offending first line with a clear,
  specific error — enforcing the intro is the intended
  behavior, not a parser fix.
- Workaround until then: prepend a non-dash intro sentence.

## Stage: trapezoid close-out

The close-out commit becomes a two-parent merge — first
parent the pre-cycle base (the parent of the ladder's first
commit), second parent the ladder tip (`-X`), the
`git merge --no-ff` convention — so
`git log --first-parent main` reads one commit per cycle
while the full ladder stays reachable via the second parent.

- The merge itself is trivial in-process: a two-parent
  commit in one transaction; no conflicts possible since the
  base is an ancestor of the tip.
- The real work is ochid stamping — the bot commit carries
  one ochid per work commit in the cycle (the AGENTS.md
  merge non-ff shape) while push today stamps only the
  topmost commit.
- Teach `validate-desc` + the push sanity verifiers the new
  legal shape (two parents, N ochids).
- End state of the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause point between
  commit and publish stages", which stays as the interim
  manual recipe until this lands.

## Stage: por → dual conversion

Attach a bot companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an
existing por workspace; emit cross-links going forward.
Manual setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
proved arduous; should be a routine subcommand. Design stub
in [[5]] § 2.

- Last on purpose: it leans on
  [facade topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology) and the
  in-process init pieces.

## Access rule

Today's implicit rule — read-only display commands use
jj-lib in-process, mutating commands shell out — dissolves
once mutations are in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the
end state instead.

## Out of scope: still spawned

- `gh` (remote provisioning) and `$EDITOR` stay spawned by
  design.
- The `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn goes away when the
  Todo "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process" lands —
  same direction, independently shippable, not part of this
  program.
- Beneficiary: the Todo "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
  invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
  in-process read.

## Design risk: op-store coexistence

We think in-process op-store writes must coexist with the
user's external `jj` CLI driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41
vs installed jj 0.40.0 format/version skew needs a
compatibility check before the
[mutation stage](#stage-jj-lib-migration) commits to the
approach.

# References

[1]: /notes/bugs.md
[2]: /notes/chores/chores-12.md#docs-pordual-parity-design-0610
[3]: /notes/chores/chores-03.md#submodule--ochid-circular-dependency-bug-0290
[4]: /notes/chores/chores-06.md#non-tracking-remote-bookmark-detection-design
[5]: /notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity.md

content.text: Now let me survey where "non-ff" / "trapezoid" appear and look at the push implementation and cycle protocol.

input.command: grep -rn --include='*.md' -i 'trapezoid\|non-ff' . | grep -v '^\./\.claude' | grep -v target/
input.description: Find all mentions of trapezoid / non-ff in docs

content.content:
AGENTS.md:227:  non-ff close-out (one ochid per Work commit in the cycle).
notes/done.md:143:- docs: codify merge-non-ff recipe — promote the merge-non-ff close-out recipe to a `### Merge non-ff recipe` subsection in cycle-protocol.md (rebase → `jj new` lift → push + post-hoc caveat); reword `### Shape at close-out push` (work-done framing, Merge non-ff tagged default); standardize jj rebase `-d` → `--onto`/`-o` in AGENTS.md and drop the post-amend `jj new` note (the recipe now owns the empty-`@` why); also clarified the Preparation step (Cargo.lock, In-Progress move wording) (0.64.0) [[110]]
notes/done.md:148:- docs: todo cleanup + trapezoid entries (0.67.1) — push-related todos reshaped around the trapezoidal (merge non-ff) workflow: new #1 bookmark-invariant fix and #2 push pause point; "record uncovered code commits (N:1)" re-scoped to code worked outside vc-x1; `push --squash` demoted to todo-backlog.md; cycle-protocol.md push-wrapper list synced [[115]]

aiTitle: Implement non-ff merge and trapezoidal close-out

content.content:
360	  permanently destroy work and aren't a normal cycle step, so the
361	  bot flags one before acting. An ordinary delegated cycle never
362	  reaches this.
363	- **Still transparent** — report each commit/push as it lands
364	  (title + outcome) so the user can catch up.
365	- **When in doubt, ask** — ambiguous authorization falls back to
366	  per-push approval.
367	
368	### Shape at close-out push
369	
370	At close-out the cycle's *work* is done; its *published
371	shape* is the remaining choice, made at push time. Surface
372	the options and get user approval before pushing — once on
373	the target, changing shape is a remote rewrite (force-push,
374	needs approval), so choose deliberately.
375	
376	- **Squash to one commit** — single entry on the target.
377	  Right for straightforward changes where the Work-N is
378	  focused on one or two files.
379	- **Merge non-ff** *(current default)* — `main` gains a
380	  merge commit (`X.Y.Z`); cycle commits stay reachable via
381	  two parents. `jj log -r ..@ -n <N>` shows the trapezoidal
382	  shape. See [Merge non-ff recipe](#merge-non-ff-recipe)
383	  for the full setup sequence.
384	- **Keep separate** — one commit per cycle entry on
385	  `main`. Use when the decomposition itself is
386	  informative. Each chores section keeps its own header /
387	  `Commits:` ref; no consolidation churn.
388	
389	Set up squash/merge before invoking `vc-x1 push`; use
390	`jj git push` directly for non-standard shapes.
391	
392	### Merge non-ff recipe
393	
394	Setting up a [Merge non-ff](#shape-at-close-out-push)
395	close-out is a fixed sequence. `<closeout>` is the cycle's
396	close-out commit, `<prev>` the previous cycle's close-out
397	(the current `main` tip), `<work-tip>` the cycle's last
398	Work commit:
399	
400	1. **Rebase the close-out into a merge** — `jj rebase -r
401	   <closeout> --onto <prev> --onto <work-tip>`.
402	   - `-r <closeout>` keeps the `<closeout>` commit in place.
403	   - `--onto <prev>` becomes its first parent (trunk).
404	   - `--onto <work-tip>` becomes the second parent.
405	
406	   Together these make `<closeout>` a merge of
407	   `<prev>` + `<work-tip>`, forming a trapezoidal commit.
408	2. **Use `jj new <merge>`** to add an empty `@` above the
409	   merge. The rebase left `@` *on* the now-content-bearing
410	   merge, which git/IDE diff views show as uncommitted;
411	   `jj new` restores the clean empty `@` on top.
412	3. **Push** — `jj git push --bookmark main -R .`.
413	
414	**Post-hoc caveat.** If the cycle was already pushed
415	[Keep separate](#shape-at-close-out-push) its commits are
416	immutable: the rebase needs `--ignore-immutable` and the
417	push force-updates `main`.
418	The standard sequence assumes the merge is set up *before*
419	the close-out push.
420	
421	### vc-x1 push wrapper
422	
423	`vc-x1 push <bookmark>` wraps per-push mechanics. See
424	`vc-x1 push --help` for current flags. `<bookmark>` names a
425	work-repo bookmark only; the bot repo is always pinned to
426	`main` (see [.claude cadence](#claude-cadence)).
427	
428	**Current limitation**: only fully supports the
429	[Keep separate](#shape-at-close-out-push) shape; other
430	shapes need manual jj steps. Planned improvements are
431	project state, tracked in the project's `TODO.md` —
432	this protocol describes only the stable mechanism.
433	
434	### .claude cadence
435	
436	**Cadence**: one push = one bot-repo commit, paired
437	with every work-repo commit in that push.
438	
439	The `.claude` working copy accumulates session data
440	across the cycle; its change ID stays stable across
441	snapshots, `jj describe`, and the squash-push fold, so
442	work-repo `ochid:` trailers resolve.
443	
444	`.claude` is a linear journal — all session work lives
445	on `main`, regardless of the work-repo bookmark. **Do
446	not create or maintain bot-repo bookmarks that mirror
447	work-repo branches** — risks the bot steering session
448	pushes to the wrong remote ref.
449	
450	Ending a session: if the user runs `/exit` there will be
451	session information created, which we don't worry about.
452	The user can close the terminal instead and `@` will
453	remain empty.
454	
455	### Bot communication at the reviews
456	
457	Use plain prose — no insider jargon ("Gate N signal",
458	"Checkpoint N", etc.):
459	
460	- **At Work review** — summarize what changed and stop.
461	  "Work complete. Please review."
462	- **At Commit Description review** — present `$TITLE`
463	  and `$BODY` explicitly; ask permission to commit/push.
464	  Don't spell out the full `vc-x1 push ... --title ...
465	  --body ...` invocation by default.
466	- **At Post close-out review** — surface the shape
467	  options (squash / merge / keep) and the push target;
468	  wait for the user's choice before any `jj squash` /
469	  `jj rebase` / `jj git push` invocation.

file.content:
permanently destroy work and aren't a normal cycle step, so the
  bot flags one before acting. An ordinary delegated cycle never
  reaches this.
- **Still transparent** — report each commit/push as it lands
  (title + outcome) so the user can catch up.
- **When in doubt, ask** — ambiguous authorization falls back to
  per-push approval.

### Shape at close-out push

At close-out the cycle's *work* is done; its *published
shape* is the remaining choice, made at push time. Surface
the options and get user approval before pushing — once on
the target, changing shape is a remote rewrite (force-push,
needs approval), so choose deliberately.

- **Squash to one commit** — single entry on the target.
  Right for straightforward changes where the Work-N is
  focused on one or two files.
- **Merge non-ff** *(current default)* — `main` gains a
  merge commit (`X.Y.Z`); cycle commits stay reachable via
  two parents. `jj log -r ..@ -n <N>` shows the trapezoidal
  shape. See [Merge non-ff recipe](#merge-non-ff-recipe)
  for the full setup sequence.
- **Keep separate** — one commit per cycle entry on
  `main`. Use when the decomposition itself is
  informative. Each chores section keeps its own header /
  `Commits:` ref; no consolidation churn.

Set up squash/merge before invoking `vc-x1 push`; use
`jj git push` directly for non-standard shapes.

### Merge non-ff recipe

Setting up a [Merge non-ff](#shape-at-close-out-push)
close-out is a fixed sequence. `<closeout>` is the cycle's
close-out commit, `<prev>` the previous cycle's close-out
(the current `main` tip), `<work-tip>` the cycle's last
Work commit:

1. **Rebase the close-out into a merge** — `jj rebase -r
   <closeout> --onto <prev> --onto <work-tip>`.
   - `-r <closeout>` keeps the `<closeout>` commit in place.
   - `--onto <prev>` becomes its first parent (trunk).
   - `--onto <work-tip>` becomes the second parent.

   Together these make `<closeout>` a merge of
   `<prev>` + `<work-tip>`, forming a trapezoidal commit.
2. **Use `jj new <merge>`** to add an empty `@` above the
   merge. The rebase left `@` *on* the now-content-bearing
   merge, which git/IDE diff views show as uncommitted;
   `jj new` restores the clean empty `@` on top.
3. **Push** — `jj git push --bookmark main -R .`.

**Post-hoc caveat.** If the cycle was already pushed
[Keep separate](#shape-at-close-out-push) its commits are
immutable: the rebase needs `--ignore-immutable` and the
push force-updates `main`.
The standard sequence assumes the merge is set up *before*
the close-out push.

### vc-x1 push wrapper

`vc-x1 push <bookmark>` wraps per-push mechanics. See
`vc-x1 push --help` for current flags. `<bookmark>` names a
work-repo bookmark only; the bot repo is always pinned to
`main` (see [.claude cadence](#claude-cadence)).

**Current limitation**: only fully supports the
[Keep separate](#shape-at-close-out-push) shape; other
shapes need manual jj steps. Planned improvements are
project state, tracked in the project's `TODO.md` —
this protocol describes only the stable mechanism.

### .claude cadence

**Cadence**: one push = one bot-repo commit, paired
with every work-repo commit in that push.

The `.claude` working copy accumulates session data
across the cycle; its change ID stays stable across
snapshots, `jj describe`, and the squash-push fold, so
work-repo `ochid:` trailers resolve.

`.claude` is a linear journal — all session work lives
on `main`, regardless of the work-repo bookmark. **Do
not create or maintain bot-repo bookmarks that mirror
work-repo branches** — risks the bot steering session
pushes to the wrong remote ref.

Ending a session: if the user runs `/exit` there will be
session information created, which we don't worry about.
The user can close the terminal instead and `@` will
remain empty.

### Bot communication at the reviews

Use plain prose — no insider jargon ("Gate N signal",
"Checkpoint N", etc.):

- **At Work review** — summarize what changed and stop.
  "Work complete. Please review."
- **At Commit Description review** — present `$TITLE`
  and `$BODY` explicitly; ask permission to commit/push.
  Don't spell out the full `vc-x1 push ... --title ...
  --body ...` invocation by default.
- **At Post close-out review** — surface the shape
  options (squash / merge / keep) and the push target;
  wait for the user's choice before any `jj squash` /
  `jj rebase` / `jj git push` invocation.

364	clones: syncing t1B after a t1A push moved `main` and `@` for
365	the first time.

file.content:
Plain `vc-x1 sync` — the invocation a user reaches for — now
converges the bookmark *and* seats `@` on it in both repos, and
a failed sync ends with inspectable state plus an explicit,
scoped undo (`vc-x1 revert`). Verified on the real test-repo-1
clones: syncing t1B after a t1A push moved `main` and `@` for
the first time.

## docs: todo cleanup + trapezoid entries (0.67.1)

Commits: [[17]]

A session on the trapezoidal-commit workflow (work a code-repo
bookmark 1:1, land it as the merge non-ff close-out) turned
into a reshape of the push-related todos. Verified that
`vc-x1 push <bookmark>` applies the one bookmark name to both
repos — violating the bot-repo-linear-on-`main` invariant the
moment a feature bookmark is pushed. Single-commit cycle.

- New Todo #1 "push/sync: bookmark is code-repo-only; pin the
  bot repo to main" — the invariant fix; prereq for working
  the code repo on feature bookmarks.
- New Todo #2 "vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and
  publish stages" — the trapezoid close-out stays 1:1: commit
  stages run normally, pause for the merge rebase (chids
  survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid), resume via
  `--from bookmark-both`; retires the manual pre-commit
  workaround.
- "vc-x1 push: record uncovered code commits (N:1 code↔bot)"
  re-scoped to code worked outside vc-x1 (no bot pairings
  exist); the trapezoid close-out is explicitly out of scope.
- "vc-x1 push --squash" demoted to todo-backlog.md —
  after-publication squash is off the routine path now that
  merge non-ff is the routine shape; pre-publication squash
  needs no tooling.
- cycle-protocol.md push-wrapper improvements list synced to
  the reshaped todos.

### As-built ladder

- 0.67.1 single-commit cycle: todo reshape + backlog demotion
  + wrapper-list sync + this section.

## feat: pin bot repo to main (0.68.0)

Commits: [[18]],[[19]],[[20]],[[21]],[[22]],[[23]]

`vc-x1 push <bookmark>` applied the one bookmark name to both
repos (preflight tracking check, `bookmark-both`,
`finalize --push`), and sync's classify/fetch used the passed
bookmark for every repo — but the bot repo is a linear journal
on `main` by design. Pushing a feature bookmark would create
and push that bookmark in the bot repo, leave the bot `main`
behind, and wedge the next sync's `reposition_session`. Prereq
for the trapezoidal-commit workflow (branch the code repo; bot
stays on `main`).

Two adjacent sync warts surfaced (and were fixed) mid-cycle
while dogfooding: the unconditional `jj new main` on the
session repo, and the three-lines-to-say-nothing clean-case
output.

### As-built ladder

- 0.68.0-0 prep: backfill Commits:, bump version, pick up
  todo, open chores section
- 0.68.0-1 sync: session repo pins `main` — tracking
  preflight + classify/act use a per-repo bookmark; tests
- 0.68.0-2 sync: `reposition_session` no-ops when `@-` is
  already the `main` tip — previously it always `jj new
  main`ed (empty `@`: chid/op churn; non-empty `@`: live
  session writes stranded on a sibling head); tests
- 0.68.0-3 sync: quiet output — clean case prints one
  summary line (`UP_TO_DATE_MSG` const shared with main.rs's
  long_about); per-repo "@ already on" no-op lines demoted
  to debug
- 0.68.0-4 push: every stage's session-repo side uses
  `main`, never the passed bookmark
  - preflight verifies the session repo tracks `main`
  - bookmark stage sets app → `<bookmark>`, session →
    `main`; renamed `bookmark-both` → `bookmark-set` (no
