1	//! `push` subcommand — collapse the dual-repo commit+push+
2	//! squash-push ceremony into a single resumable command.
3	//!
4	//! See `notes/chores/chores-05.md > Add push subcommand (0.37.0)` for the
5	//! full design.
6	//!
7	//! Dev-step ladder (expanded from original 4 to 6 after adding an
8	//! integration-test step ahead of the first dogfood):
9	//!
10	//! - `0.37.0-0` — scaffolding: flag surface, `Stage` enum, stub `push()`
11	//! - `0.37.0-1` — state file + stage-dispatch loop with stage stubs;
12	//!   `--status`, `--restart`, `--from`
13	//! - `0.37.0-2` — real stage bodies (commits, bookmarks, push,
14	//!   session push) + `jj op` snapshot rollback
15	//! - `0.37.0-3` — integration tests + workspace-root refactor
16	//!   (thread `root: &Path` through every stage so fixtures can
17	//!   point them at tempdirs); first `vc-x1 push` dogfood ships
18	//!   this commit
19	//! - `0.37.0-4` — interactivity: two approval gates, `$EDITOR`,
20	//!   message persistence across resumes
21	//! - `0.37.0-5` — polish: `--dry-run`, `--step`, non-tty handling,
22	//!   `.gitignore` coherence warning
23	//! - `0.37.0` — docs + workflow migration (done marker)
24	
25	use std::fs;
26	use std::io::IsTerminal;
27	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
28	
29	use chrono::Utc;
30	use clap::{Args, ValueEnum};
31	use log::{debug, info, warn};
32	
33	use crate::common::{prompt, run};
34	use crate::context::Context;
35	use crate::options_flags::squash::SquashSpec;
36	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
37	use crate::sync::{current_op_id, op_restore};
38	use crate::toml_simple::toml_load;
39	
40	/// Bookmark the bot (`.claude`) repo always advances and
41	/// pushes. The bot repo is a linear journal on `main` by
42	/// design — `<bookmark>` names a work-repo bookmark only.
43	const BOT_BOOKMARK: &str = "main";
44	
45	/// Named stages of the `push` state machine.
46	///
47	/// Used by `--from <stage>` to resume at a specific point and by
48	/// `--status` to report the current position. Ordered top-down so
49	/// `Stage as u8` comparisons reflect progress through the flow.
50	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, ValueEnum)]
51	#[value(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
52	pub enum Stage {
53	    /// Verify bookmark tracking, the bot-published invariant,
54	    /// and sync state.
55	    Preflight,
56	    /// Present diff for the first approval gate.
57	    Review,
58	    /// Compose / edit the commit message; present for second gate.
59	    Message,
60	    /// Commit the work repo.
61	    CommitWork,
62	    /// Commit the `.claude` bot repo (skipped if empty).
63	    CommitBot,
64	    /// Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: work → `<bookmark>`,
65	    /// bot → `main`.
66	    BookmarkSet,
67	    /// `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`.
68	    PushWork,
69	    /// In-process squash of `.claude`'s trailing session writes
70	    /// + push `main`.
71	    SquashPushBot,
72	}
73	
74	impl Stage {
75	    /// Return the stage's kebab-case string identifier (matches what
76	    /// the CLI accepts via `--from <stage>` and what `PushState`
77	    /// persists to disk).
78	    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
79	        match self {
80	            Stage::Preflight => "preflight",
81	            Stage::Review => "review",
82	            Stage::Message => "message",
83	            Stage::CommitWork => "commit-work",
84	            Stage::CommitBot => "commit-bot",
85	            Stage::BookmarkSet => "bookmark-set",
86	            Stage::PushWork => "push-work",
87	            Stage::SquashPushBot => "squash-push-bot",
88	        }
89	    }
90	
91	    /// Parse a kebab-case stage name back into a `Stage`.
92	    ///
93	    /// Unknown names return `None`; callers should surface a helpful
94	    /// error rather than silently substituting a default.
95	    pub fn from_str(name: &str) -> Option<Self> {
96	        match name {
97	            "preflight" => Some(Stage::Preflight),
98	            "review" => Some(Stage::Review),
99	            "message" => Some(Stage::Message),
100	            "commit-work" => Some(Stage::CommitWork),
101	            "commit-bot" => Some(Stage::CommitBot),
102	            "bookmark-set" => Some(Stage::BookmarkSet),
103	            "push-work" => Some(Stage::PushWork),
104	            "squash-push-bot" => Some(Stage::SquashPushBot),
105	            _ => None,
106	        }
107	    }
108	
109	    /// The stage that follows this one, or `None` when this is the
110	    /// last stage (`SquashPushBot`).
111	    pub fn next(self) -> Option<Self> {
112	        match self {
113	            Stage::Preflight => Some(Stage::Review),
114	            Stage::Review => Some(Stage::Message),
115	            Stage::Message => Some(Stage::CommitWork),
116	            Stage::CommitWork => Some(Stage::CommitBot),
117	            Stage::CommitBot => Some(Stage::BookmarkSet),
118	            Stage::BookmarkSet => Some(Stage::PushWork),
119	            Stage::PushWork => Some(Stage::SquashPushBot),
120	            Stage::SquashPushBot => None,
121	        }
122	    }
123	
124	    /// The first stage in the flow — used when no saved state exists.
125	    pub fn first() -> Self {
126	        Stage::Preflight
127	    }
128	}
129	
130	/// CLI arguments for the `push` subcommand.
131	///
132	/// Flag set mirrors the design in `notes/chores/chores-05.md`. Flags are
133	/// parsed in 0.37.0-0; their real effects land across the remaining
134	/// 0.37.0-N steps. See the module docstring for which flags activate
135	/// when.
136	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
137	pub struct PushArgs {
138	    /// Bookmark to advance in the work repo (positional form of
139	    /// `--bookmark`). The bot repo always advances `main`.
140	    ///
141	    /// Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
142	    /// without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an
143	    /// alias for scripts and for composition with other args. The two
144	    /// forms conflict if both supplied.
145	    #[arg(value_name = "BOOKMARK", conflicts_with = "bookmark")]
146	    pub bookmark_pos: Option<String>,
147	
148	    /// Bookmark to advance in the work repo (flag form; see positional).
149	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "bookmark_pos")]
150	    pub bookmark: Option<String>,
151	
152	    /// Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1.
153	    #[arg(long)]
154	    pub restart: bool,
155	
156	    /// Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use).
157	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STAGE")]
158	    pub from: Option<Stage>,
159	
160	    /// Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate.
161	    #[arg(long)]
162	    pub step: bool,
163	
164	    /// Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit.
165	    #[arg(long)]
166	    pub status: bool,
167	
168	    /// Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run succeeded).
169	    #[arg(long)]
170	    pub recheck: bool,
171	
172	    /// Stop before `squash-push-bot` so it can be run manually.
173	    #[arg(long)]
174	    pub no_squash_push: bool,
175	
176	    /// Print the exact commands for every stage without side effects.
177	    #[arg(long)]
178	    pub dry_run: bool,
179	
180	    /// Commit title (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage).
181	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STR")]
182	    pub title: Option<String>,
183	
184	    /// Commit body (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage).
185	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STR")]
186	    pub body: Option<String>,
187	
188	    /// Auto-approve interactive prompts (review gate, message-edit
189	    /// confirmation). Required when `--title` / `--body` aren't both
190	    /// supplied in non-interactive contexts.
191	    #[arg(short = 'y', long)]
192	    pub yes: bool,
193	}
194	
195	/// Inputs to the push op, flat, owned, clap-free.
196	///
197	/// Mirrors `PushArgs`, with the two clap bookmark spellings
198	/// (positional `BOOKMARK` and `--bookmark`) collapsed into one
199	/// `bookmark` field. The rest map straight over: `restart`, `from`
200	/// (a `Stage`, a domain type — kept), `step`, `status`, `recheck`,
201	/// `no_squash_push`, `dry_run`, `title`, `body`, `yes`.
202	pub struct PushParams {
203	    pub bookmark: Option<String>,
204	    pub restart: bool,
205	    pub from: Option<Stage>,
206	    pub step: bool,
207	    pub status: bool,
208	    /// `--recheck` is parsed by `PushArgs` but not yet wired into the
209	    /// stage machine; carried here so the conversion stays total and
210	    /// the field is in place when the behavior lands.
211	    #[allow(dead_code)]
212	    pub recheck: bool,
213	    pub no_squash_push: bool,
214	    pub dry_run: bool,
215	    pub title: Option<String>,
216	    pub body: Option<String>,
217	    pub yes: bool,
218	}
219	
220	impl From<&PushArgs> for PushParams {
221	    /// Convert clap-derived `PushArgs` into the flat `PushParams`,
222	    /// collapsing `bookmark_pos` / `bookmark` (clap already enforces
223	    /// they don't both appear) into the single `bookmark` field.
224	    fn from(a: &PushArgs) -> Self {
225	        Self {
226	            bookmark: a.bookmark_pos.clone().or_else(|| a.bookmark.clone()),
227	            restart: a.restart,
228	            from: a.from,
229	            step: a.step,
230	            status: a.status,
231	            recheck: a.recheck,
232	            no_squash_push: a.no_squash_push,
233	            dry_run: a.dry_run,
234	            title: a.title.clone(),
235	            body: a.body.clone(),
236	            yes: a.yes,
237	        }
238	    }
239	}
240	
241	impl SubcommandRunner for PushArgs {
242	    type Params = PushParams;
243	
244	    /// Delegate to the existing `From<&PushArgs>` impl above
245	    /// (total — never fails).
246	    fn to_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String> {
247	        Ok(PushParams::from(self))
248	    }
249	
250	    /// Run the existing `push` op.

1119	fn stage_commit_work(
1120	    root: &Path,
1121	    state: &PushState,
1122	    params: &PushParams,
1123	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1124	    let (title, body) = resolve_message(state, params)?;
1125	    let bot_chid = state
1126	        .bot_chid
1127	        .as_deref()
1128	        .ok_or("push commit-work: bot_chid not set (message stage didn't run)")?;
1129	    let body_with_trailer = format!("{body}\n\nochid: /.claude/{bot_chid}");
1130	    let work_arg = root.to_string_lossy();
1131	    if params.dry_run {
1132	        info!(
1133	            "push commit-work: [dry-run] would run jj commit -R {work_arg} -m \"{title}\" -m <body+ochid>"
1134	        );
1135	        return Ok(());
1136	    }
1137	    info!("push commit-work: jj commit -R {work_arg}");
1138	    run(
1139	        "jj",
1140	        &[
1141	            "commit",
1142	            "-R",
1143	            &work_arg,
1144	            "-m",
1145	            &title,
1146	            "-m",
1147	            &body_with_trailer,
1148	        ],
1149	        root,
1150	    )?;
1151	    Ok(())
1152	}
1153	
1154	/// Pull the title/body pair from CLI args or persisted state,
1155	/// preferring CLI args (override-on-resume case). Returns `Err`
1156	/// when neither source has them — which only happens if
1157	/// `stage_message` didn't run or was force-bypassed.
1158	fn resolve_message(
1159	    state: &PushState,
1160	    params: &PushParams,
1161	) -> Result<(String, String), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1162	    let title = params
1163	        .title
1164	        .clone()
1165	        .or_else(|| state.title.clone())
1166	        .ok_or("push: title missing — message stage didn't run")?;
1167	    let body = params
1168	        .body
1169	        .clone()
1170	        .or_else(|| state.body.clone())
1171	        .ok_or("push: body missing — message stage didn't run")?;
1172	    Ok((title, body))
1173	}
1174	
1175	/// Commit `.claude` with the same title/body and the ochid trailer
1176	/// pointing at the work commit's chid, or skip if `.claude` had no
1177	/// pending changes.
1178	fn stage_commit_bot(
1179	    root: &Path,
1180	    state: &PushState,
1181	    params: &PushParams,
1182	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1183	    if !state.bot_had_changes.unwrap_or(false) {
1184	        info!("push commit-bot: skip (.claude had no pending changes)");
1185	        return Ok(());
1186	    }
1187	    let (title, body) = resolve_message(state, params)?;
1188	    let work_chid = state
1189	        .work_chid
1190	        .as_deref()
1191	        .ok_or("push commit-bot: work_chid not set (message stage didn't run)")?;
1192	    let body_with_trailer = format!("{body}\n\nochid: /{work_chid}");
1193	    let claude = claude_path(root);
1194	    let claude_arg = claude.to_string_lossy();
1195	    if params.dry_run {
1196	        info!(
1197	            "push commit-bot: [dry-run] would run jj commit -R {claude_arg} -m \"{title}\" -m <body+ochid>"
1198	        );
1199	        return Ok(());
1200	    }
1201	    info!("push commit-bot: jj commit -R {claude_arg}");
1202	    run(
1203	        "jj",
1204	        &[
1205	            "commit",
1206	            "-R",
1207	            &claude_arg,
1208	            "-m",
1209	            &title,
1210	            "-m",
1211	            &body_with_trailer,
1212	        ],
1213	        root,
1214	    )?;
1215	    Ok(())
1216	}
1217	
1218	/// Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: the work repo advances
1219	/// `state.bookmark`, the bot repo always advances
1220	/// `BOT_BOOKMARK` (`main`) — a feature bookmark must never be
1221	/// created in the linear-journal bot repo.
1222	fn stage_bookmark_set(
1223	    root: &Path,
1224	    state: &PushState,
1225	    params: &PushParams,
1226	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1227	    let bk = &state.bookmark;
1228	    let work_arg = root.to_string_lossy();
1229	    let claude = claude_path(root);
1230	    let claude_arg = claude.to_string_lossy();
1231	    if params.dry_run {
1232	        info!(
1233	            "push bookmark-set: [dry-run] would run jj bookmark set {bk} -r @- -R {work_arg} / {BOT_BOOKMARK} -r @- -R {claude_arg}"
1234	        );
1235	        return Ok(());
1236	    }
1237	    info!(
1238	        "push bookmark-set: jj bookmark set {bk} -r @- -R {work_arg} / {BOT_BOOKMARK} -r @- -R {claude_arg}"
1239	    );
1240	    run(
1241	        "jj",
1242	        &["bookmark", "set", bk, "-r", "@-", "-R", &work_arg],
1243	        root,
1244	    )?;
1245	    run(
1246	        "jj",
1247	        &[
1248	            "bookmark",
1249	            "set",
1250	            BOT_BOOKMARK,
1251	            "-r",
1252	            "@-",
1253	            "-R",
1254	            &claude_arg,
1255	        ],
1256	        root,
1257	    )?;
1258	    Ok(())
1259	}
1260	
1261	/// Push the work repo's bookmark to origin.
1262	fn stage_push_work(
1263	    root: &Path,
1264	    state: &PushState,
1265	    params: &PushParams,
1266	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1267	    let bk = &state.bookmark;
1268	    let work_arg = root.to_string_lossy();
1269	    if params.dry_run {
1270	        info!("push push-work: [dry-run] would run jj git push --bookmark {bk} -R {work_arg}");
1271	        return Ok(());
1272	    }
1273	    info!("push push-work: jj git push --bookmark {bk} -R {work_arg}");
1274	    run(
1275	        "jj",
1276	        &["git", "push", "--bookmark", bk, "-R", &work_arg],
1277	        root,
1278	    )?;
1279	    Ok(())
1280	}
1281	
1282	/// Squash-push `.claude` in-process, always pushing
1283	/// `BOT_BOOKMARK` (`main`) — the bot repo's bookmark is
1284	/// pinned, so `state.bookmark` plays no part here.
1285	///
1286	/// - Squashes the tail (session writes that landed since
1287	///   `commit-bot`) into the session commit, then pushes — via
1288	///   `squash_push::squash_push`, so the ochid-drop guard
1289	///   applies and a failure is a visible push failure.
1290	/// - In-process since 0.69.0-1 (the stage's detached child died
1291	///   silently at sandbox teardown — the loss diagnosed in
1292	///   0.68.1).
1293	/// - `--no-squash-push` turns this stage into a no-op so the
1294	///   squash+push can be run manually.
1295	fn stage_squash_push_bot(
1296	    root: &Path,
1297	    _state: &PushState,
1298	    params: &PushParams,
1299	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1300	    if params.no_squash_push {
1301	        info!("push squash-push-bot: skip (--no-squash-push)");
1302	        return Ok(());
1303	    }
1304	    let bk = BOT_BOOKMARK;
1305	    let claude = claude_path(root);
1306	    let claude_arg = claude.to_string_lossy();
1307	    if params.dry_run {
1308	        info!(
1309	            "push squash-push-bot: [dry-run] would squash @ → @- and push {bk} in {claude_arg} (in-process)"
1310	        );
1311	        return Ok(());
1312	    }
1313	    info!("push squash-push-bot: squash @ → @- + push {bk} -R {claude_arg} (in-process)");
1314	    let sp = crate::squash_push::SquashPushParams {
1315	        repo: claude.clone(),
1316	        squash: SquashSpec {
1317	            source: "@".to_string(),
1318	            target: "@-".to_string(),
1319	        },
1320	        bookmark: bk.to_string(),
1321	        // Mid-push the mismatch is the normal state (bookmark-set
1322	        // just moved main; this stage publishes it) — don't report
1323	        // a lost publish.
1324	        report_publish_state: false,
1325	    };
1326	    crate::squash_push::squash_push(&sp)
1327	}
1328	
1329	/// Verify the saved state still matches reality before any stage runs.
1330	///
1331	/// Catches the failure mode where out-of-band activity (manual
1332	/// squash-push, squash + force-push, abandon, etc.) moved the world
1333	/// forward between
1334	/// the prior push and this resume, leaving `state` pointing at a
1335	/// now-bogus halt point. The 0.37.1 dogfood incident is the canonical
1336	/// example: push parked at its last stage after a failure; manual
1337	/// recovery moved the world forward; the next push resumed at the
1338	/// parked stage, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed all stages"
1339	/// while working copies still held uncommitted changes.
1340	///
1341	/// Three checks (per chores-06 [61] design):
1342	///
1343	/// 1. `state.work_chid` still resolves in the work repo (not abandoned).
1344	/// 2. After `bookmark-set` has run (state.stage ∈ {PushWork,
1345	///    SquashPushBot}), the bookmark points at a commit whose chid
1346	///    matches `state.work_chid`.
1347	/// 3. `state.bot_chid` still resolves in `.claude`.
1348	///
1349	/// On any mismatch: error with the exact `vc-x1 push <bookmark>
1350	/// --restart` remediation. A fresh state (no chids yet) is a no-op —
1351	/// nothing to verify.
1352	fn verify_state_sanity(root: &Path, state: &PushState) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1353	    let Some(work_chid) = &state.work_chid else {
1354	        return Ok(()); // OK: fresh state, nothing to verify yet
1355	    };
1356	    let bookmark = &state.bookmark;
1357	    let root_str = root.to_string_lossy();
1358	    let cwd = Path::new(".");
1359	
1360	    // 1. work_chid still resolves.
1361	    run(
1362	        "jj",
1363	        &[
1364	            "log",
1365	            "-r",
1366	            work_chid,
1367	            "--no-graph",
1368	            "-T",
1369	            "\"x\"",
1370	            "-R",
1371	            &root_str,
1372	        ],
1373	        cwd,
1374	    )
1375	    .map_err(|_| -> Box<dyn std::error::Error> {
1376	        format!(
1377	            "state-sanity: work_chid '{work_chid}' has been abandoned or no longer resolves \
1378	             in the work repo. State is stale — run `vc-x1 push {bookmark} --restart` to clear."
1379	        )
1380	        .into()
1381	    })?;
1382	
1383	    // 2. bookmark at work_chid (after bookmark-set has run).
1384	    if matches!(state.stage, Stage::PushWork | Stage::SquashPushBot) {
1385	        let bookmark_chid = run(
1386	            "jj",
1387	            &[
1388	                "log",
1389	                "-r",
1390	                bookmark,
1391	                "--no-graph",
1392	                "-T",
1393	                "change_id.short(12)",
1394	                "-R",
1395	                &root_str,
1396	            ],
1397	            cwd,
1398	        )?;

content.content:
111	## Preparation
112	
113	The cycle's first commit (`X.Y.Z-0`), when the cycle needs
114	setup (a lightweight cycle omits it — see
115	[versioning.md](versioning.md#step-numbering)):
116	
117	- **Bump the version-of-record** to `X.Y.Z-0`. Where it
118	  lives, the suffix scheme, and any derived files (a
119	  lockfile, a sourced manifest version) are
120	  project-specific — see [versioning.md](versioning.md).
121	- **Move a `## Todo` item** (if the cycle has one) into
122	  `## In Progress` and the todo item should have:
123	  - A **bold title line** — that will be the chores
124	    section header, minus the `## ` prefix.
125	  - A **succinct problem statement**; add if one is needed
126	  - A **plan ladder**.
127	- **Open the [chores section](#chores-sections)** —
128	  append a `##` header with the title it records — the
129	  cycle's anticipated close-out title.
130	
131	## Work-N
132	
133	The cycle's work commits (`X.Y.Z-1`, `X.Y.Z-2`, …)
134	implement the change. As many as needed:
135	
136	- Each commit runs through the
137	  **[per-commit flow](#per-commit-flow)**.
138	- **Interim pushes** are optional (backup, progress
139	  visibility).
140	- Close-out is the only mandatory push (see
141	  [Pushing](#pushing)).
142	- **Subdivide into a sub-cycle** if a Work commit's
143	  scope grows enough (see
144	  [Sub-cycle ladders](#sub-cycle-ladders)).
145	
146	## Close-out
147	
148	The cycle's last commit (bare `X.Y.Z`) does bookkeeping
149	only — the commit body describes that bookkeeping, not
150	what happens post-squash:
151	
152	- **Move the picked-up item** from `## In Progress` to a
153	  one-line entry (with a chores `[N]` ref) in `## Done`.
154	- **Finalize the chores section** (opened during Preparation
155	  and grown per commit — see [Chores sections](#chores-sections)):
156	  - The problem statement is already the chores intro (written
157	    when the section was opened) and the plan ladder is already
158	    the `### As-built ladder` (a rung appended per commit), so
159	    close-out only *finalizes*, it does not cut-and-paste.
160	  - Sync the chores header to the **final** commit title if the
161	    cycle's scope shifted; update every anchor back-reference.
162	  - Add any `### design subsections`; optional `### Outcome`
163	    notes.
164	  - Replace the `## In Progress` cycle block with
165	    `_No cycle currently in progress._`.
166	- **Update `notes/README.md`** if functionality changed
167	  (new flags, new subcommands, changed behavior).
168	
169	Whether to **squash** the cycle into one commit before the
170	publishing push, or push as-is, is decided at push time —
171	see [Pushing](#pushing).
172	
173	## Numbering
174	
175	Each commit's phase is encoded in the version suffix — `-0`
176	Preparation, `-1`/`-2`/… Work, bare `X.Y.Z` Close-out,
177	recursively for sub-cycles. The full scheme — disambiguation,
178	nesting, optional Preparation, the project's version-of-record
179	format, and the per-phase bump — lives in
180	[versioning.md](versioning.md#step-numbering), which is the
181	single source of truth for this repo's versioning.
182	
183	## Per-commit flow
184	
185	Every commit (Preparation, each Work commit, Close-out) goes

## Preparation

The cycle's first commit (`X.Y.Z-0`), when the cycle needs
setup (a lightweight cycle omits it — see
[versioning.md](versioning.md#step-numbering)):

- **Bump the version-of-record** to `X.Y.Z-0`. Where it
  lives, the suffix scheme, and any derived files (a
  lockfile, a sourced manifest version) are
  project-specific — see [versioning.md](versioning.md).
- **Move a `## Todo` item** (if the cycle has one) into
  `## In Progress` and the todo item should have:
  - A **bold title line** — that will be the chores
    section header, minus the `## ` prefix.
  - A **succinct problem statement**; add if one is needed
  - A **plan ladder**.
- **Open the [chores section](#chores-sections)** —
  append a `##` header with the title it records — the
  cycle's anticipated close-out title.

## Work-N

The cycle's work commits (`X.Y.Z-1`, `X.Y.Z-2`, …)
implement the change. As many as needed:

- Each commit runs through the
  **[per-commit flow](#per-commit-flow)**.
- **Interim pushes** are optional (backup, progress
  visibility).
- Close-out is the only mandatory push (see
  [Pushing](#pushing)).
- **Subdivide into a sub-cycle** if a Work commit's
  scope grows enough (see
  [Sub-cycle ladders](#sub-cycle-ladders)).

## Close-out

The cycle's last commit (bare `X.Y.Z`) does bookkeeping
only — the commit body describes that bookkeeping, not
what happens post-squash:

- **Move the picked-up item** from `## In Progress` to a
  one-line entry (with a chores `[N]` ref) in `## Done`.
- **Finalize the chores section** (opened during Preparation
  and grown per commit — see [Chores sections](#chores-sections)):
  - The problem statement is already the chores intro (written
    when the section was opened) and the plan ladder is already
    the `### As-built ladder` (a rung appended per commit), so
    close-out only *finalizes*, it does not cut-and-paste.
  - Sync the chores header to the **final** commit title if the
    cycle's scope shifted; update every anchor back-reference.
  - Add any `### design subsections`; optional `### Outcome`
    notes.
  - Replace the `## In Progress` cycle block with
    `_No cycle currently in progress._`.
- **Update `notes/README.md`** if functionality changed
  (new flags, new subcommands, changed behavior).

Whether to **squash** the cycle into one commit before the
publishing push, or push as-is, is decided at push time —
see [Pushing](#pushing).

## Numbering

Each commit's phase is encoded in the version suffix — `-0`
Preparation, `-1`/`-2`/… Work, bare `X.Y.Z` Close-out,
recursively for sub-cycles. The full scheme — disambiguation,
nesting, optional Preparation, the project's version-of-record
format, and the per-phase bump — lives in
[versioning.md](versioning.md#step-numbering), which is the
single source of truth for this repo's versioning.

## Per-commit flow

Every commit (Preparation, each Work commit, Close-out) goes

content.content:
234	## Commit description
235	
236	[Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/):
237	
238	```
239	<type>: <short description>
240	```
241	
242	Titles carry **no trailing `(<version>)` suffix**. The
243	version-of-record — where it lives and its bump cadence, see
244	[versioning.md](versioning.md) — is useful for confirming
245	you're running the version you're testing, not a per-commit
246	title marker. We think per-commit version-in-title is
247	unstable on large projects: many changes are in flight
248	simultaneously, and a
249	version is only stable once merged into the main repo — so
250	titles don't carry one.
251	
252	### Title
253	
254	- ≤50 chars total.
255	- Common types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `test`,
256	  `docs`, `chore`.
257	- Favor terse phrasings.
258	- **Distinct per step** — each of a cycle's commits gets its
259	  own descriptive title (no shared cycle title with a step
260	  marker). Share a greppable stem across the cycle's titles
261	  (e.g. `ring buffer`) so `git log --grep` collects them; the
262	  chores section header matches the close-out title. See
263	  AGENTS.md
264	  [Conventional-commit shape](../AGENTS.md#conventional-commit-shape-ladder--chores--commit).
265	
266	### Body
267	
268	[Prose form](../AGENTS.md#prose-form) (intro + bullets),
269	wrap ≤72. Bullet content differs per repo:
270	
271	- **Work-repo body**: file-by-file. One bullet per file
272	  changed (file plus a one-line gist). Sub-bullets for
273	  files with multiple distinct changes:
274	
275	  ```
276	  - path/to/file1
277	    - first distinct change
278	    - second distinct change, wrapping to next line
279	       with continuation indented 5
280	  ```
281	
282	  The file-by-file list is the source of truth for the
283	  cycle's mechanical change record. Chores carries the
284	  narrative + design, not a copy of it. Promote any
285	  "why" beyond one sentence to a chores `###` subsection.
286	
287	- **Bot-repo (`.claude`) body**: bullets describe
288	  in-session activity rather than work-repo changes.
289	
290	### Trailer
291	
292	`ochid:` as the last line of the body — see
293	[Cross-repo linking (ochid trailers)](../AGENTS.md#cross-repo-linking-ochid-trailers)
294	in AGENTS.md for the convention.
295	
296	For breaking changes, use the hyphenated `BREAKING-CHANGE:`
297	trailer key. `BREAKING CHANGE:` (with a space) is the only
298	space-separated key the Conventional Commits spec allows; the
299	hyphenated form is also valid and avoids the space ambiguity.
300	
301	## Reviewing changes
302	
303	Work review looks at the **uncommitted working-copy diff**,
304	on the way to commit. The user opens diffs in their
305	editor (Zed, VSCode); jj commands are for terminal:
306	
307	- `jj diff` — working-copy diff (uncommitted)
308	- `jj diff -r @-` — diff of the previous commit
309	- `jj diff --from <X> --to <Y>` — any two revisions
310	- `jj show -r <X>` — description + diff for one rev
311	
312	Don't `jj edit -r @-` to view a past commit — that marks
313	it mutable and shifts `@`; use `jj diff -r @-` or
314	`jj show -r @-`.
315	
316	See [Sub-cycle ladders](#sub-cycle-ladders) for the
317	close-out squash recipe and recovery; revset primitives
318	are in [`jj-tips.md`](jj-tips.md#revsets).
319	
320	## Pushing
321	
322	### Policy
323	
324	Push is **discretionary** during the cycle (backup,
325	progress visibility) and **mandatory at close-out** —
326	the cycle's result must be published.
327	
328	**Approval is per-push.** Every push — any repo, any kind:
329	cycle push, interim backup, recovery/surgery force-push —
330	happens only after the user has reviewed the changes to be
331	published and explicitly approved that specific push.
332	Approval of a plan that *includes* a push does not authorize
333	the push itself; stop and ask again at the moment of pushing.
334	
335	**Default is interactive; an explicit scoped delegation waives
336	the gates.** The gates above — per-push approval, the
337	commit-description review (show title+body and stop), and the
338	hard stop after push/squash-push — are the *interactive
339	default*.
340	They yield when the user **explicitly** delegates a complete,
341	bounded task and authorizes carrying it through ("do all of X
342	and push each step, don't check in"). The bot then proceeds
343	through that task's commits and pushes without stopping, and
344	continues past each push to the next step. Conditions:
345	
346	- **Explicit grant** — never inferred from a task merely being
347	  well-scoped; the user's words must authorize unattended
348	  completion. "Commit and push" (or "then push") names the
349	  destination, not a waiver: it authorizes the push *after*
350	  the normal work review and description review, not skipping
351	  them. Only wording that explicitly waives the stops ("don't
352	  check in", "no need to review", "carry it through
353	  unattended") waives them.
354	- **Bounded goal** — covers the named task only; does not carry
355	  to the next task or a vaguer follow-on.
356	- **Destructive ops still pause** — delegation covers the task's
357	  ordinary commits and pushes; it does *not* pre-authorize a
358	  genuinely irreversible action (force-push over published
359	  history, history rewrite, deleting a remote branch). Those can
360	  permanently destroy work and aren't a normal cycle step, so the
361	  bot flags one before acting. An ordinary delegated cycle never
362	  reaches this.
363	- **Still transparent** — report each commit/push as it lands

file.content:
## Commit description

[Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/):

```
<type>: <short description>
```

Titles carry **no trailing `(<version>)` suffix**. The
version-of-record — where it lives and its bump cadence, see
[versioning.md](versioning.md) — is useful for confirming
you're running the version you're testing, not a per-commit
title marker. We think per-commit version-in-title is
unstable on large projects: many changes are in flight
simultaneously, and a
version is only stable once merged into the main repo — so
titles don't carry one.

### Title

- ≤50 chars total.
- Common types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `test`,
  `docs`, `chore`.
- Favor terse phrasings.
- **Distinct per step** — each of a cycle's commits gets its
  own descriptive title (no shared cycle title with a step
  marker). Share a greppable stem across the cycle's titles
  (e.g. `ring buffer`) so `git log --grep` collects them; the
  chores section header matches the close-out title. See
  AGENTS.md
  [Conventional-commit shape](../AGENTS.md#conventional-commit-shape-ladder--chores--commit).

### Body

[Prose form](../AGENTS.md#prose-form) (intro + bullets),
wrap ≤72. Bullet content differs per repo:

- **Work-repo body**: file-by-file. One bullet per file
  changed (file plus a one-line gist). Sub-bullets for
  files with multiple distinct changes:

  ```
  - path/to/file1
    - first distinct change
    - second distinct change, wrapping to next line
       with continuation indented 5
  ```

  The file-by-file list is the source of truth for the
  cycle's mechanical change record. Chores carries the
  narrative + design, not a copy of it. Promote any
  "why" beyond one sentence to a chores `###` subsection.

- **Bot-repo (`.claude`) body**: bullets describe
  in-session activity rather than work-repo changes.

### Trailer

`ochid:` as the last line of the body — see
[Cross-repo linking (ochid trailers)](../AGENTS.md#cross-repo-linking-ochid-trailers)
in AGENTS.md for the convention.

For breaking changes, use the hyphenated `BREAKING-CHANGE:`
trailer key. `BREAKING CHANGE:` (with a space) is the only
space-separated key the Conventional Commits spec allows; the
hyphenated form is also valid and avoids the space ambiguity.

## Reviewing changes

Work review looks at the **uncommitted working-copy diff**,
on the way to commit. The user opens diffs in their
editor (Zed, VSCode); jj commands are for terminal:

- `jj diff` — working-copy diff (uncommitted)
- `jj diff -r @-` — diff of the previous commit
- `jj diff --from <X> --to <Y>` — any two revisions
- `jj show -r <X>` — description + diff for one rev

Don't `jj edit -r @-` to view a past commit — that marks
it mutable and shifts `@`; use `jj diff -r @-` or
`jj show -r @-`.

See [Sub-cycle ladders](#sub-cycle-ladders) for the
close-out squash recipe and recovery; revset primitives
are in [`jj-tips.md`](jj-tips.md#revsets).

## Pushing

### Policy

Push is **discretionary** during the cycle (backup,
progress visibility) and **mandatory at close-out** —
the cycle's result must be published.

**Approval is per-push.** Every push — any repo, any kind:
cycle push, interim backup, recovery/surgery force-push —
happens only after the user has reviewed the changes to be
published and explicitly approved that specific push.
Approval of a plan that *includes* a push does not authorize
the push itself; stop and ask again at the moment of pushing.

**Default is interactive; an explicit scoped delegation waives
the gates.** The gates above — per-push approval, the
commit-description review (show title+body and stop), and the
hard stop after push/squash-push — are the *interactive
default*.
They yield when the user **explicitly** delegates a complete,
bounded task and authorizes carrying it through ("do all of X
and push each step, don't check in"). The bot then proceeds
through that task's commits and pushes without stopping, and
continues past each push to the next step. Conditions:

- **Explicit grant** — never inferred from a task merely being
  well-scoped; the user's words must authorize unattended
  completion. "Commit and push" (or "then push") names the
  destination, not a waiver: it authorizes the push *after*
  the normal work review and description review, not skipping
  them. Only wording that explicitly waives the stops ("don't
  check in", "no need to review", "carry it through
  unattended") waives them.
- **Bounded goal** — covers the named task only; does not carry
  to the next task or a vaguer follow-on.
- **Destructive ops still pause** — delegation covers the task's
  ordinary commits and pushes; it does *not* pre-authorize a
  genuinely irreversible action (force-push over published
  history, history rewrite, deleting a remote branch). Those can
  permanently destroy work and aren't a normal cycle step, so the
  bot flags one before acting. An ordinary delegated cycle never
  reaches this.
- **Still transparent** — report each commit/push as it lands

54	## Unique per commit (preference, not requirement)
55	
56	Our general notion is that the version-of-record should change
57	on **every commit**, so a built or running artifact identifies
58	the exact commit it came from.
59	
60	- This is a preference, **not** a hard requirement. A project
61	  following the cycle protocol may bump less often — once per
62	  cycle, only at release, and so on. Record the choice in
63	  [Recording the version-of-record](#recording-the-version-of-record)
64	  if it differs.
65	- It is achievable because the cycle's versions (below) are
66	  **pre-assignable**, unlike the git SHA, which a commit cannot
67	  contain (see the cycle protocol's
68	  [Commits backfill](cycle-protocol.md#commits-backfill)).
69	
70	## Step numbering
71	
72	The cycle (Preparation -> Work -> Close-out; see
73	[cycle-protocol.md](cycle-protocol.md)) encodes each commit's
74	phase in the version suffix — the **final identifier `0` marks
75	a Preparation**:
76	
77	- `X.Y.Z-0` — Preparation
78	- `X.Y.Z-1`, `X.Y.Z-2`, … — Work commits
79	- `X.Y.Z` — Close-out (bare version, no suffix)
80	
81	**Preparation is optional.** A lightweight cycle — no ladder,
82	no setup commit — skips `-0` and starts at `-1` (its first
83	Work commit). The same holds at every level: a sub-cycle
84	needing no Preparation omits its `.0` (see Nesting). One that
85	grows a Preparation later adds the `0` step without
86	renumbering siblings.
87	
88	Disambiguation:
89	
90	- `-10` — Work commit #10 (final identifier `10`), not a
91	  Preparation.
92	- `-1.0` — Preparation of the `-1` sub-cycle (final
93	  identifier `0`).
94	
95	**Nesting.** Sub-cycles append another level, recursively:
96	
97	- `X.Y.Z-3.0` — Preparation of the `-3` sub-cycle
98	- `X.Y.Z-3.1`, `X.Y.Z-3.2` — its Work

file.content:
## Unique per commit (preference, not requirement)

Our general notion is that the version-of-record should change
on **every commit**, so a built or running artifact identifies
the exact commit it came from.

- This is a preference, **not** a hard requirement. A project
  following the cycle protocol may bump less often — once per
  cycle, only at release, and so on. Record the choice in
  [Recording the version-of-record](#recording-the-version-of-record)
  if it differs.
- It is achievable because the cycle's versions (below) are
  **pre-assignable**, unlike the git SHA, which a commit cannot
  contain (see the cycle protocol's
  [Commits backfill](cycle-protocol.md#commits-backfill)).

## Step numbering

The cycle (Preparation -> Work -> Close-out; see
[cycle-protocol.md](cycle-protocol.md)) encodes each commit's
phase in the version suffix — the **final identifier `0` marks
a Preparation**:

- `X.Y.Z-0` — Preparation
- `X.Y.Z-1`, `X.Y.Z-2`, … — Work commits
- `X.Y.Z` — Close-out (bare version, no suffix)

**Preparation is optional.** A lightweight cycle — no ladder,
no setup commit — skips `-0` and starts at `-1` (its first
Work commit). The same holds at every level: a sub-cycle
needing no Preparation omits its `.0` (see Nesting). One that
grows a Preparation later adds the `0` step without
renumbering siblings.

Disambiguation:

- `-10` — Work commit #10 (final identifier `10`), not a
  Preparation.
- `-1.0` — Preparation of the `-1` sub-cycle (final
  identifier `0`).

**Nesting.** Sub-cycles append another level, recursively:

- `X.Y.Z-3.0` — Preparation of the `-3` sub-cycle
- `X.Y.Z-3.1`, `X.Y.Z-3.2` — its Work

397	  wording was retired (0.71.0-7). In-view column labeling
398	  (headers / a legend) left as an optional nicety
399	- docs: shared protocol sync + jj refactor plan — adopted the vc-template-x1 shared notes set (AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, versioning.md, jj-tips.md) with vc-x1's 0.69.0 corrections ratified template-side (manifest: [notes-sync-20260716.md](notes/notes-sync-20260716.md)); jj facade → jj-lib refactor program planned in [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md), absorbing eight Todos [[1]]
400	- docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md — todo list moved from notes/ to the conventional root-file family; live references swept (AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, README, ARCHITECTURE, notes/*); no-arg validate-todo / fix-todo default follows the move; historical files keep `notes/todo.md`; the shared doc set diverges until vc-template-x1 and iiac-perf apply the same change [[2]]
401	- feat: bot-session --col-width knob — the field views'
402	  (`--fields`/`--unknown`/`--per-line`) first-column pad
403	  becomes `--col-width N`, default widened 44 → 68 (aligns
404	  the type column for ~99% of observed key paths; only the
405	  long-tail `snapshot.trackedFileBackups.<abs path>.*` keys
406	  overflow); config-hierarchy resolution deferred to Todo #12 [[6]]
407	- feat: bot-session --fields + --raw explorer — bot-session
408	  doubles as a schema explorer: --fields (dotted-path
409	  inventory per entry type: count, kinds, samples),
410	  --unknown (inventory minus the extractor's KNOWN_PATHS —
411	  the unmodeled surface; 132 paths on first real run),
412	  --raw (pretty-printed source lines); --per-line (a fields
413	  section per source line, composes with --unknown); --lines
414	  unified to source-JSONL-line units in every view,
415	  conversation included; drift-over-time baseline deferred to the
416	  discovery/index cycle [[5]]
417	- feat: bot-session --result-lines knob — the [result]-body
418	  cap becomes a flag: `--result-lines N` (default 10, 0 =
419	  unlimited), Output-range help group; was hardwired to 10
420	  even under --all [[4]]
421	- feat: bot-session transcript viewer — display a session transcript as a conversation: two-layer tolerant parse (serde_json text → Value; hand extraction into our structs, raw retained), eight-item composable output (--<item> / --no-<item> / --all / --none) with git-style config defaults (CLI > .vc-config.toml > user config > built-in), --lines slicing, UTC headers; --raw and index view deferred (Todo #12), EPIPE logger panic recorded (Bugs #4) [[3]]
422

input.new_string:
**feat: push merge close-out (trapezoid)** — teach
`vc-x1 push` the Merge non-ff close-out shape natively.
The trapezoid is the default published shape for a
multi-commit cycle, but push only supports Keep separate:
today's close-out pre-commits both sides manually, rebases
the code close-out into the merge, and resumes via
`--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
that inject `ochid:` trailers.
   - Pulled forward from the refactor program's
     [trapezoid close-out stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out),
     spawn-based for now — the in-process merge transaction
     folds into the jj-lib migration stage.
   - Absorbs the retired Todo "vc-x1 push: pause point
     between commit and publish stages" — the pause was the
     interim manual path for the same close-out; native
     merge support supersedes it unimplemented.
   - Folds in the retired Ideas "Codify ochid invariant …"
     codification remnants at the docs step (ochid
     invariant, bot-repo rules, Ideas-aware
     Preparation/Close-out); its cross-repo migration
     sketch moved to todo-backlog.md.
   - Design points for the merge stage:
     - merge sits after commit-bot, before bookmark-set —
       chids survive the rebase, so both ochid directions
       stay valid uninjected-order-free
     - mechanics: `jj rebase -r <closeout> --onto <target
       bookmark> --onto <work tip>` then `jj new <merge>`
       to re-seat an empty `@`
     - preconditions: target is an ancestor of the work
       tip; close-out has exactly one parent pre-rebase;
       degenerate single-commit cycle errors (plain push)
     - ochid list: one `ochid:` per work commit the push
       newly publishes (held-local cycle: whole ladder +
       merge); whether to skip commits already covered by
       an interim 1:1 push decided at the stamping step
       (cheap bot-journal trailer scan exists in
       `squash_push::extract_ochids`)
   - Ladder:
     - 0.72.0-0 prep: version bump, todo pickup + triage,
       open chores section (current)
     - 0.72.0-1 refactor: extract push/state.rs — the
       refactor program's split-push.rs stage pulled
       forward (Stage, PushState, state layout) so the
       merge stage lands in a file that reviews cleanly
     - 0.72.0-2 push: --merge flag + merge-work stage —
       two-parent rebase, `jj new` re-seat, state
       persistence/resume/--status/--dry-run/rollback;
       integration test of the shape
     - 0.72.0-3 push: N-ochid stamping — commit-bot emits
       one `ochid:` per newly published work commit; tests
     - 0.72.0-4 validators: validate-desc + push sanity
       verifiers learn the merge shape (two parents, N
       ochids); one string-level multi-ochid parser
     - 0.72.0-5 docs: cycle-protocol.md (Merge non-ff
       recipe → wrapper flag, wrapper-limitation note,
       ochid invariant + bot-repo rules codified),
       notes/README.md, refactor doc stage status
     - 0.72.0 close-out and validation — dogfood: this
       cycle lands via `push --merge`

input.old_string:
1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
   sequence:
   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
     stages, ochids injected both directions)
   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
   - publish

   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
     normally, before the rebase.
   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
     operation).
   - Interim recipe only: the refactor program's
     [trapezoid close-out stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out)
     is the end state; this pause point remains the manual
     path until it lands.

80	## Todo
81	
82	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
83	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
84	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
85	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
86	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
87	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
88	 number. Long-tail entries
89	 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
90	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
91	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
92	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
93	
94	1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
95	   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
96	   sequence:
97	   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
98	     stages, ochids injected both directions)
99	   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
100	     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
101	   - publish
102	
103	   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
104	   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
105	   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
106	   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
107	   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
108	     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
109	     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
110	   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
111	     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
112	     normally, before the rebase.
113	   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
114	     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
115	     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
116	     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
117	     operation).
118	   - Interim recipe only: the refactor program's
119	     [trapezoid close-out stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out)
120	     is the end state; this pause point remains the manual
121	     path until it lands.
122	
123	2. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
124	   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
125	   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
126	   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
127	   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
128	   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
129	   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md))
130	   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
131	   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
132	   former Todos live in
133	   [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md).
134	   - Stages in execution order: DRY facade → hygiene
135	     riders → facade owns topology → de-gitify init →
136	     split push.rs → jj-lib migration → push body-intro
137	     validation → trapezoid close-out → por → dual
138	     conversion.
139	3. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
140	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
141	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
142	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
143	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
144	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.

file.content:
## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
   sequence:
   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
     stages, ochids injected both directions)
   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
   - publish

   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
     normally, before the rebase.
   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
     operation).
   - Interim recipe only: the refactor program's
     [trapezoid close-out stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out)
     is the end state; this pause point remains the manual
     path until it lands.

2. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md))
   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
   former Todos live in
   [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md).
   - Stages in execution order: DRY facade → hygiene
     riders → facade owns topology → de-gitify init →
     split push.rs → jj-lib migration → push body-intro
     validation → trapezoid close-out → por → dual
     conversion.
3. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.

47. **`vc-x1 clone`: graceful single-repo fallback when no
    companion `.claude` remote.** Default dual clone errors
    mid-way when `<source>.claude` doesn't exist; `--por` is
    the workaround (works, but you must know to pass it).
    - After the code clone, probe the session URL with
      `git ls-remote <url> HEAD`; on failure report "no
      companion session repo — cloning code repo only"
      (GitHub reports missing and no-access identically) and
      skip the bot clone + symlink; done message says single
      repo.
    - A clone failure after a successful probe stays a real
      error.
    - Dry-run text notes steps 2/3 are skipped when no
      companion exists.
    - Integration-testable offline via path-form sources
      (code bare repo present, no `.claude` bare repo).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process (2026-07-16)

The plan-and-design document for the jj refactor program.
Version-control operations are scattered hand-rolled
`run("jj", …)` spawns (~30 query sites plus every mutation)
with per-module private wrappers, plus raw-git vestiges in
init. Spawning means stderr parsing instead of typed errors,
and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside
our control — the push `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]] can't
be retried where it fails.

How this file works:

- Each stage below is a `##` section in execution order;
  each is roughly one `X.Y.Z` cycle. Section anchors are the
  durable way to reference a stage from TODO.md, chores, or
  bugs.md.
- This file owns the forward plan and design. When a stage
  ships, its `chores-NN.md` section records the commits and
  narrative as usual; the stage section here gets a one-line
  status link to that chores section — no duplication.
- The `## Todo` entry "Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib
  in-process; end subprocess spawning" in
  [TODO.md](../TODO.md) is the short pointer here.

Absorbed former Todos: "Dedup jj subprocess queries behind a
typed facade", "Split push.rs state machinery", "vc-x1 push:
validate body opens with an intro paragraph", "Make the bot
repo directory name configurable", "Terminology stragglers
from the work/bot sweep", "single-field `options_flags`
leaves → `value` field", "`por → dual` conversion",
"`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization".

## Stage: DRY facade

~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
"--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
module has quietly grown a private wrapper. Spotted at
0.69.0-2: `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind" but is
the Nth reinvention.

- One facade module (name open: `src/jj.rs` or a `common`
  split): `jj_log(repo, rev, template)` plus typed helpers —
  `rev_exists`, `chid_of`, `cid_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty`.
- Existing reinventions to fold:
  `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
  rev_is_empty_undescribed}`, `push::jj_log_empty` + four
  inline `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
  three template variants in `sync.rs`.
- Fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw `std::process::Command` +
  its own `@origin:` prefix parse) onto
  `common::verify_tracking`'s parser — two tracking parsers
  will eventually disagree.
- Unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
  `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs `common::extract_ochid`
  (jj-lib `Commit`-based); one string-level parser in
  `desc_helpers` both can call.
- Test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` / `cid()` /
  `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
  `push/integration_tests.rs`, `sync/integration_tests.rs`,
  and `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`.

## Stage: hygiene riders

Sweeps that churn the same lines the facade stage rewrites;
done early so later stages diff cleanly.

- Terminology stragglers from the 0.69.0-4 work/bot sweep:
  - `-s` scope keyword `code` (renaming to `work` is a
    breaking CLI change; `bot` already matches)
  - init's literal remote names `remote-code.git` /
    `remote-claude.git`
  - identifiers `derive_session_url`, `claude_path`,
    `Fixture.claude`, `Side::Code`
  - decide rename vs document-as-historical (overlaps
    [Stage: facade owns topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology))
- Single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field (the
  0.47.0 convention from the `squash` leaf): sweep `repo`,
  `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`
  + consumers (`init.rs`, tests). Open: could a single field
  be a type/enum instead of a struct, eliminating the
  `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` naming issue entirely.

## Stage: facade owns topology

Repo resolution becomes facade state, not per-command
preludes.

- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization: replace the
  `other_repo_from_config` prelude in both
  (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`) with
  scope-aware resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot` when
  absent; body unchanged. The 0.61.0 audit [[2]] called this
  the cheapest equalization and the prototype for the
  topology-from-config rule (topology from `default_scope`,
  not a flag); the remaining implementation gaps live in
  [[2]]'s `## Gap list`.
- Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
  hardcoded `.claude`; decided 2026-07-15 the name need not
  be `.claude`). The literal is embedded across
  init/clone/push/sync, `claude_path()`, ochid path
  semantics (`/.claude/<chid>`), the
  `~/.claude/projects/<path>` symlink, and docs;
  `.vc-config.toml` already records workspace paths — the
  natural home. Open: migration of existing workspaces, the
  ochid prefix recorded in immutable history, the
  harness-controlled symlink location.

## Stage: de-gitify init

`init.rs::push_repo`'s strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate
flow is the step shape the abandoned submodule design
required [[3]]; the symlink design doesn't need jj evicted.

- jj-only replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
  scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates the
  git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
  `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
  `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set main
  -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`.
- No `--allow-new` needed; push established tracking as a
  side effect (matches the test-fixture observation in
  [[4]]).
- Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
  `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.

## Stage: split push.rs

`push.rs` (~1.6k lines, the largest file) holds the `Stage`
machine, TOML state persistence, eight stage bodies, two
sanity verifiers, and the interactive gates; `sync/state.rs`
is the in-repo precedent.

- Extract `push/state.rs` (`Stage`, `PushState`, state
  layout resolution) so the
  [jj-lib migration](#stage-jj-lib-migration) rewrites stage
  bodies in a file that reviews cleanly.

## Stage: jj-lib migration

Migrate the facade internals to jj-lib (already a dependency
for read-only commands); removes jj and git spawning
entirely.

- Reads first: `jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors.
- Then mutations: commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
  fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store lift.
- The index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
  retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for the
  bugs.md `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]].
- `LocalBareInit`'s `git init --bare` becomes a gix call
  (jj-lib already pulls gix in).

## Stage: push body-intro validation

A bullet-first commit body trips jj's arg parser today
(`-m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a stray flag; hit on
0.62.0-5) and push fails with an opaque error.

- Validate the body opens with a non-dash intro line (Prose
  Form) and report the offending first line with a clear,
  specific error — enforcing the intro is the intended
  behavior, not a parser fix.
- Workaround until then: prepend a non-dash intro sentence.

## Stage: trapezoid close-out

The close-out commit becomes a two-parent merge — first
parent the pre-cycle base (the parent of the ladder's first
commit), second parent the ladder tip (`-X`), the
`git merge --no-ff` convention — so
`git log --first-parent main` reads one commit per cycle
while the full ladder stays reachable via the second parent.

- The merge itself is trivial in-process: a two-parent
  commit in one transaction; no conflicts possible since the
  base is an ancestor of the tip.
- The real work is ochid stamping — the bot commit carries
  one ochid per work commit in the cycle (the AGENTS.md
  merge non-ff shape) while push today stamps only the
  topmost commit.
- Teach `validate-desc` + the push sanity verifiers the new
  legal shape (two parents, N ochids).
- End state of the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause point between
  commit and publish stages", which stays as the interim
  manual recipe until this lands.

## Stage: por → dual conversion

Attach a bot companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an
existing por workspace; emit cross-links going forward.
Manual setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
proved arduous; should be a routine subcommand. Design stub
in [[5]] § 2.

- Last on purpose: it leans on
  [facade topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology) and the
  in-process init pieces.

## Access rule

Today's implicit rule — read-only display commands use
jj-lib in-process, mutating commands shell out — dissolves
once mutations are in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the
end state instead.

## Out of scope: still spawned

- `gh` (remote provisioning) and `$EDITOR` stay spawned by
  design.
- The `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn goes away when the
  Todo "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process" lands —
  same direction, independently shippable, not part of this
  program.
- Beneficiary: the Todo "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
  invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
  in-process read.

## Design risk: op-store coexistence

We think in-process op-store writes must coexist with the
user's external `jj` CLI driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41
vs installed jj 0.40.0 format/version skew needs a
compatibility check before the
[mutation stage](#stage-jj-lib-migration) commits to the
approach.

# References

[1]: /notes/bugs.md
[2]: /notes/chores/chores-12.md#docs-pordual-parity-design-0610
[3]: /notes/chores/chores-03.md#submodule--ochid-circular-dependency-bug-0290
[4]: /notes/chores/chores-06.md#non-tracking-remote-bookmark-detection-design
[5]: /notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity.md

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process (2026-07-16)

The plan-and-design document for the jj refactor program.
Version-control operations are scattered hand-rolled
`run("jj", …)` spawns (~30 query sites plus every mutation)
with per-module private wrappers, plus raw-git vestiges in
init. Spawning means stderr parsing instead of typed errors,
and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition sits outside
our control — the push `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]] can't
be retried where it fails.

How this file works:

- Each stage below is a `##` section in execution order;
  each is roughly one `X.Y.Z` cycle. Section anchors are the
  durable way to reference a stage from TODO.md, chores, or
  bugs.md.
- This file owns the forward plan and design. When a stage
  ships, its `chores-NN.md` section records the commits and
  narrative as usual; the stage section here gets a one-line
  status link to that chores section — no duplication.
- The `## Todo` entry "Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib
  in-process; end subprocess spawning" in
  [TODO.md](../TODO.md) is the short pointer here.

Absorbed former Todos: "Dedup jj subprocess queries behind a
typed facade", "Split push.rs state machinery", "vc-x1 push:
validate body opens with an intro paragraph", "Make the bot
repo directory name configurable", "Terminology stragglers
from the work/bot sweep", "single-field `options_flags`
leaves → `value` field", "`por → dual` conversion",
"`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization".

## Stage: DRY facade

~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
"--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
module has quietly grown a private wrapper. Spotted at
0.69.0-2: `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind" but is
the Nth reinvention.

- One facade module (name open: `src/jj.rs` or a `common`
  split): `jj_log(repo, rev, template)` plus typed helpers —
  `rev_exists`, `chid_of`, `cid_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty`.
- Existing reinventions to fold:
  `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
  rev_is_empty_undescribed}`, `push::jj_log_empty` + four
  inline `change_id.short(12)` blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
  three template variants in `sync.rs`.
- Fold `main::bm_track_one` (raw `std::process::Command` +
  its own `@origin:` prefix parse) onto
  `common::verify_tracking`'s parser — two tracking parsers
  will eventually disagree.
- Unify string-level ochid trailer parsing:
  `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs `common::extract_ochid`
  (jj-lib `Commit`-based); one string-level parser in
  `desc_helpers` both can call.
- Test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` / `cid()` /
  `chid()` / `description()` helpers from
  `push/integration_tests.rs`, `sync/integration_tests.rs`,
  and `tests/cli_sync.rs` into `test_helpers.rs`.

## Stage: hygiene riders

Sweeps that churn the same lines the facade stage rewrites;
done early so later stages diff cleanly.

- Terminology stragglers from the 0.69.0-4 work/bot sweep:
  - `-s` scope keyword `code` (renaming to `work` is a
    breaking CLI change; `bot` already matches)
  - init's literal remote names `remote-code.git` /
    `remote-claude.git`
  - identifiers `derive_session_url`, `claude_path`,
    `Fixture.claude`, `Side::Code`
  - decide rename vs document-as-historical (overlaps
    [Stage: facade owns topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology))
- Single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field (the
  0.47.0 convention from the `squash` leaf): sweep `repo`,
  `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`
  + consumers (`init.rs`, tests). Open: could a single field
  be a type/enum instead of a struct, eliminating the
  `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` naming issue entirely.

## Stage: facade owns topology

Repo resolution becomes facade state, not per-command
preludes.

- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` por equalization: replace the
  `other_repo_from_config` prelude in both
  (`validate_desc.rs:133`, `fix_desc.rs:152`) with
  scope-aware resolution that no-ops `Side::Bot` when
  absent; body unchanged. The 0.61.0 audit [[2]] called this
  the cheapest equalization and the prototype for the
  topology-from-config rule (topology from `default_scope`,
  not a flag); the remaining implementation gaps live in
  [[2]]'s `## Gap list`.
- Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
  hardcoded `.claude`; decided 2026-07-15 the name need not
  be `.claude`). The literal is embedded across
  init/clone/push/sync, `claude_path()`, ochid path
  semantics (`/.claude/<chid>`), the
  `~/.claude/projects/<path>` symlink, and docs;
  `.vc-config.toml` already records workspace paths — the
  natural home. Open: migration of existing workspaces, the
  ochid prefix recorded in immutable history, the
  harness-controlled symlink location.

## Stage: de-gitify init

`init.rs::push_repo`'s strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate
flow is the step shape the abandoned submodule design
required [[3]]; the symlink design doesn't need jj evicted.

- jj-only replacement verified on jj 0.40.0 (2026-07-16,
  scratch run): `jj git init --colocate <dest>` (creates the
  git repo itself — the explicit `git init` in
  `prepare_local_repo` is redundant), `jj commit`,
  `jj git remote add origin <url>`, `jj bookmark set main
  -r @-`, `jj git push --bookmark main`.
- No `--allow-new` needed; push established tracking as a
  side effect (matches the test-fixture observation in
  [[4]]).
- Collapses Step 7/Step 10; drops `git clean` /
  `git checkout` / `git remote add` / `git push -u`.

## Stage: split push.rs

`push.rs` (~1.6k lines, the largest file) holds the `Stage`
machine, TOML state persistence, eight stage bodies, two
sanity verifiers, and the interactive gates; `sync/state.rs`
is the in-repo precedent.

- Extract `push/state.rs` (`Stage`, `PushState`, state
  layout resolution) so the
  [jj-lib migration](#stage-jj-lib-migration) rewrites stage
  bodies in a file that reviews cleanly.

## Stage: jj-lib migration

Migrate the facade internals to jj-lib (already a dependency
for read-only commands); removes jj and git spawning
entirely.

- Reads first: `jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors.
- Then mutations: commit, describe, bookmark set/track,
  fetch, push — the workspace/transaction/op-store lift.
- The index-lock retry loop becomes ours (catch, back off,
  retry) with real error types — the fix vehicle for the
  bugs.md `bookmark-set` lock race [[1]].
- `LocalBareInit`'s `git init --bare` becomes a gix call
  (jj-lib already pulls gix in).

## Stage: push body-intro validation

A bullet-first commit body trips jj's arg parser today
(`-m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a stray flag; hit on
0.62.0-5) and push fails with an opaque error.

- Validate the body opens with a non-dash intro line (Prose
  Form) and report the offending first line with a clear,
  specific error — enforcing the intro is the intended
  behavior, not a parser fix.
- Workaround until then: prepend a non-dash intro sentence.

## Stage: trapezoid close-out

The close-out commit becomes a two-parent merge — first
parent the pre-cycle base (the parent of the ladder's first
commit), second parent the ladder tip (`-X`), the
`git merge --no-ff` convention — so
`git log --first-parent main` reads one commit per cycle
while the full ladder stays reachable via the second parent.

- The merge itself is trivial in-process: a two-parent
  commit in one transaction; no conflicts possible since the
  base is an ancestor of the tip.
- The real work is ochid stamping — the bot commit carries
  one ochid per work commit in the cycle (the AGENTS.md
  merge non-ff shape) while push today stamps only the
  topmost commit.
- Teach `validate-desc` + the push sanity verifiers the new
  legal shape (two parents, N ochids).
- Status: pulled forward (with
  [split push.rs](#stage-split-pushrs)) into the 0.72.0
  "feat: push merge close-out (trapezoid)" cycle,
  spawn-based — the in-process merge transaction folds into
  the [jj-lib migration](#stage-jj-lib-migration) stage.
  The former enabler Todo "vc-x1 push: pause point between
  commit and publish stages" was retired unimplemented;
  native merge support supersedes the pause.

## Stage: por → dual conversion

Attach a bot companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an
existing por workspace; emit cross-links going forward.
Manual setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
proved arduous; should be a routine subcommand. Design stub
in [[5]] § 2.

- Last on purpose: it leans on
  [facade topology](#stage-facade-owns-topology) and the
  in-process init pieces.

## Access rule

Today's implicit rule — read-only display commands use
jj-lib in-process, mutating commands shell out — dissolves
once mutations are in-process; ARCHITECTURE.md states the
end state instead.

## Out of scope: still spawned

- `gh` (remote provisioning) and `$EDITOR` stay spawned by
  design.
- The `vc-x1 sync --check` self-spawn goes away when the
  Todo "sync follow-up: push preflight in-process" lands —
  same direction, independently shippable, not part of this
  program.
- Beneficiary: the Todo "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1
  invocation" — its per-invocation jj spawn becomes an
  in-process read.

## Design risk: op-store coexistence

We think in-process op-store writes must coexist with the
user's external `jj` CLI driving the same repos; jj-lib 0.41
vs installed jj 0.40.0 format/version skew needs a
compatibility check before the
[mutation stage](#stage-jj-lib-migration) commits to the
approach.

# References

[1]: /notes/bugs.md
[2]: /notes/chores/chores-12.md#docs-pordual-parity-design-0610
[3]: /notes/chores/chores-03.md#submodule--ochid-circular-dependency-bug-0290
[4]: /notes/chores/chores-06.md#non-tracking-remote-bookmark-detection-design
[5]: /notes/design-cli/por-dual-parity.md

94	1. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
95	   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
96	   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
97	   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
98	   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed

100	   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md))

105	   - Stages in execution order: DRY facade → hygiene

114	2. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
115	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
116	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
117	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
118	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
119	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
120	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
121	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
122	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
123	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
124	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is

147	   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.

154	   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
155	   single-mode sync cycle.
