- Reads first: `jj log` templates become `Commit` accessors
  (the `src/jj.rs` facade internals; `common::load_repo` /
  `resolve_revset` are the existing in-process machinery).
  Caveat: queries against `@` rely on the spawned CLI's
  working-copy auto-snapshot — push asks "uncommitted
  changes *right now*?" — so the in-process form must
  snapshot first, and a snapshot is an op-store write:
  those reads move with the mutation lift, not before.

content.text:
The plain version: yes, the library can answer every one of these questions without launching `jj` — and that switch is a planned stage. Today's commit deliberately just funnels all thirty call sites through one narrow doorway so the switch later is a one-file job. The one genuine blocker to switching now: asking about *uncommitted* changes only works because launching `jj` quietly records the working copy's current state first; doing that ourselves in-process is a history-writing operation that belongs with the bigger migration step.

attachment.snippet:
1	//! Typed facade over `jj` subprocess queries.
2	//!
3	//! Every read-only `jj log -T <template>` / `jj bookmark list`
4	//! spawn goes through this module — primitives plus typed
5	//! helpers — so call sites stop hand-rolling argument lists (the
6	//! DRY-facade stage of `notes/refactor-20260716.md`):
7	//!
8	//! - `log` — the raw primitive; the helpers below cover the
9	//!   common templates.
10	//! - `matches` / `rev_exists` — does a revset match / does a
11	//!   revision resolve.
12	//! - `chid_of` / `cid_of` / `cid_short_of` — change / commit ids.
13	//! - `desc_of` / `is_empty` — description and emptiness.
14	//!
15	//! Mutations still spawn `jj` at their call sites; the jj-lib
16	//! migration stage moves both in-process.
17	
18	use std::path::Path;
19	
20	use crate::common::run;
21	
22	/// Crate-standard boxed-error result, aliased locally for brevity.
23	type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, Box<dyn std::error::Error>>;
24	
25	/// Run `jj log -r <rev> --no-graph -T <template> -R <repo>` and
26	/// return its stdout (`run` trims surrounding whitespace).
27	pub fn log(repo: &Path, rev: &str, template: &str) -> Result<String> {
28	    run(
29	        "jj",
30	        &[
31	            "log",
32	            "-r",
33	            rev,
34	            "--no-graph",
35	            "-T",
36	            template,
37	            "-R",
38	            &repo.to_string_lossy(),
39	        ],
40	        Path::new("."),
41	    )
42	}
43	
44	/// True when `revset` matches at least one commit in `repo`.
45	///
46	/// A valid-but-empty revset (e.g. `conflicts()` on a clean repo)
47	/// is `Ok(false)`; an unresolvable revision is an `Err` — use
48	/// `rev_exists` to fold that case to `false`.
49	pub fn matches(repo: &Path, revset: &str) -> Result<bool> {
50	    Ok(!log(repo, revset, "\"x\"")?.is_empty())
51	}
52	
53	/// True when `rev` resolves in `repo`.
54	///
55	/// Maps jj's unresolvable-revision errors (`doesn't exist`,
56	/// `No such revision`) to `Ok(false)`; other failures (bad repo
57	/// path, spawn error) stay `Err`.
58	pub fn rev_exists(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<bool> {
59	    match matches(repo, rev) {
60	        Ok(found) => Ok(found),
61	        Err(e) => {
62	            let msg = e.to_string();
63	            if msg.contains("doesn't exist") || msg.contains("No such revision") {
64	                Ok(false)
65	            } else {
66	                Err(e)
67	            }
68	        }
69	    }
70	}
71	
72	/// The 12-character change id of `rev`.
73	pub fn chid_of(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<String> {
74	    log(repo, rev, "change_id.short(12)")
75	}
76	
77	/// The full commit id of `rev`.
78	pub fn cid_of(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<String> {
79	    log(repo, rev, "commit_id")
80	}
81	
82	/// The 12-character commit id of `rev`.
83	pub fn cid_short_of(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<String> {
84	    log(repo, rev, "commit_id.short(12)")
85	}
86	
87	/// The full description (title + body) of `rev`.
88	pub fn desc_of(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<String> {
89	    log(repo, rev, "description")
90	}
91	
92	/// True when `rev` is empty (no file changes relative to its
93	/// parent). Strict: anything but `true` / `false` from the
94	/// template is an error.
95	pub fn is_empty(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<bool> {
96	    let out = log(repo, rev, "empty")?;
97	    match out.as_str() {
98	        "true" => Ok(true),
99	        "false" => Ok(false),
100	        other => Err(format!("jj::is_empty: unexpected template output {other:?}").into()),
101	    }
102	}
103	
104	/// Run `jj bookmark list <bookmark> -R <repo>` and return its
105	/// stdout — empty when the local bookmark doesn't exist.
106	pub fn bookmark_list(repo: &Path, bookmark: &str) -> Result<String> {
107	    run(
108	        "jj",
109	        &["bookmark", "list", bookmark, "-R", &repo.to_string_lossy()],
110	        Path::new("."),
111	    )
112	}
113	
114	/// Like `bookmark_list` but with `-a`: includes remote refs —
115	/// tracked ones indented (`  @origin: …`), non-tracking at
116	/// column 0 (`<bookmark>@<remote>: …`). The input for
117	/// `common::find_tracked_remote` /
118	/// `common::find_non_tracking_remote`.
119	pub fn bookmark_list_all(repo: &Path, bookmark: &str) -> Result<String> {
120	    run(
121	        "jj",
122	        &[
123	            "bookmark",
124	            "list",
125	            "-a",
126	            bookmark,
127	            "-R",
128	            &repo.to_string_lossy(),
129	        ],
130	        Path::new("."),
131	    )
132	}

78	
79	1. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
80	   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
81	   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
82	   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
83	   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
84	   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
85	   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md))
86	   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
87	   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
88	   former Todos live in
89	   [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md).
90	   - Stages in execution order: DRY facade → hygiene
91	     riders → facade owns topology → de-gitify init →
92	     split push.rs → stateless push → jj-lib migration →
93	     push body-intro validation → trapezoid close-out →
94	     por → dual conversion.
95	   - split push.rs is built and parked on
96	     `support-trapezoid-commits` (0.72.0-1) with the paused
97	     merge close-out cycle — replay or redo when the stage
98	     arrives; see the stage notes in the program doc.
99	2. **ochid: bot-repo location qualifier.** An ochid is
100	   workspace-relative (`/.claude/<chid>`) — nothing in a
101	   published commit says *where* the companion bot repo
102	   lives (vc-x1's is `github.com/winksaville/vc-x1.claude`,
103	   discoverable only by convention). Anyone cloning just the
104	   work repo can't resolve bot-side ochids. Design already
105	   sketched in forks-multi-user.md
106	   [Per-user bot repos via URL-shaped ochid](notes/forks-multi-user.md#per-user-bot-repos-via-url-shaped-ochid):
107	   URL-shaped trailers, plus the complementary
108	   `.vc-config.toml` repo-index form; resolver dispatch is
109	   one rule (URL → fetch, else workspace-relative), existing
110	   path-form trailers stay the backward-compatible case.
111	   - Cheap first rung: declare the companion's URL once in
112	     the committed `.vc-config.toml` (no trailer-format
113	     change; any work-repo clone then knows where the bot
114	     repo lives). Rides naturally with the refactor
115	     program's facade-owns-topology stage
116	     (bot-repo-location config).
117	   - Link rot + mirroring mitigations are in the same doc
118	     section.
119	3. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
120	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
121	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
122	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
123	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
124	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
125	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
126	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
127	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
128	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
129	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
130	   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
131	   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
132	   the cycle after 0.65.2.
133	   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
134	     not a pre-assigned version?
135	     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
136	       A contributors email address would do it, but also
137	       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
138	       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
139	       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
140	   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
141	     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
142	     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
143	     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.
144	4. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
145	   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
146	   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
147	   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
148	   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
149	   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
150	     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
151	     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
152	   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
153	   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
154	5. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
155	   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
156	   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass

# Chores-14

Continuation of `chores-13.md` (closed at `0.72.x`, parked on
the `support-trapezoid-commits` bookmark). This file covers
the jj refactor program
([refactor-20260716.md](../refactor-20260716.md)) from
`0.73.0` onward, worked on the `refactor-vc-x1` bookmark
while `main` parks at the `0.71.0` tip.

Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`AGENTS.md`](../../AGENTS.md#reference-numbering); chores-14
starts at `[1]`.

## refactor: DRY jj facade

Commits: see [As-built ladder](#as-built-ladder)

~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
"--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
module has quietly grown a private wrapper — spotted at
0.69.0-2, where `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind"
but was the Nth reinvention. One typed facade module ends
the reinvention; first stage of the refactor program.

### As-built ladder

- [[1]] 0.73.0-0 chore: open jj facade cycle
  - restore the five notes files from
    `support-trapezoid-commits` (TODO.md, todo-backlog,
    forks-multi-user, refactor doc, chores-13) so the shared
    bookkeeping rides the refactor line
  - re-plan after the pivot: `## In Progress` → the DRY
    facade ladder; the trapezoid `--merge [<base>]` design
    folded into the refactor doc's trapezoid stage; new
    "stateless push" stage (retire the push state file —
    from the extraction review's "why does `Stage` live in
    a file?")
  - shared-doc-sync todos updated: vc-template-x1 synced
    2026-07-20; family grows to five (adds zc-msg-x1,
    tprobe)
  - version 0.73.0-0; backfill the 0.72.0-1 ref [57] in
    chores-13; open this file
- [[2]] 0.73.0-1 refactor: jj facade query module
  - new `src/jj.rs`: the `log(repo, rev, template)` primitive
    plus typed helpers — `matches` / `rev_exists`, `chid_of`,
    `cid_of` / `cid_short_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty` — every
    read-only `jj log -T` spawn now goes through it
  - folded: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
    rev_is_empty_undescribed}` (the last two deleted, the
    empty-undescribed check rebuilt on `is_empty` +
    `desc_of`), `push::{get_change_id, jj_log_empty}`, the
    sanity verifiers' inline template blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
    `sync::{commit_id, revset_nonempty}` + the
    `commit_ids_of_bookmark` template, and
    `common::bookmark_publish_state`'s commit-id closure
  - `rev_exists` folds jj's unresolvable-revision errors to
    `false` (the `try_commit_id` patterns); the push
    stale-state checks keep their any-failure-is-stale
    behavior via `.unwrap_or(false)` at the call sites

[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/f761e89092df "f761e89092dfbb82e8ab355d6e5a058e77b07e23"

Everything's in place for `-2`. The proposed commit:

**Title:**
```
refactor: jj facade tracking parse
```

**Body:**
```
Unify the two bookmark-tracking parsers on one listing — the
DRY-facade stage's bm_track_one fold.

- src/jj.rs: `bookmark_list` / `bookmark_list_all` join the
  facade (module intro widened beyond log templates)
- src/common.rs: new `find_tracked_remote` (positive parse:
  indented `@remote` entry, synced or divergent-decorated)
  beside `find_non_tracking_remote` (negative parse) over
  the same `-a` listing; `verify_tracking` queries via the
  facade
- src/main.rs: `bm_track_one` drops its raw
  `std::process::Command` spawn + private `--tracked` parse
  — now a thin wrapper over `jj::bookmark_list_all` +
  `common::find_tracked_remote`
- src/squash_push.rs: bookmark-existence check via
  `jj::bookmark_list`; stale `cwd` binding dropped
- src/common/tests.rs: five `find_tracked_remote` tests
- TODO.md: In Progress ladder rungs now carry prepended
  `[[N]]` commit refs, backfilled once pushed (the chores
  As-built pattern); intro template documents it; refs
  [8]/[9] filled for -0/-1; stale `(current)` on the -0
  rung fixed; -2 flipped to done
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.73.0-2
- notes/chores/chores-14.md: -2 As-built rung; backfill the
  0.73.0-1 `Commits:` ref [2]
```

490 tests pass (5 new), clippy clean, installed at `0.73.0-2`. OK to push to `refactor-vc-x1`?

content.text:
All bookkeeping done, validates clean. The proposed commit:

**Title:**
```
refactor: jj facade ochid parse
```

**Body:**
```
One string-level ochid trailer parser — the DRY-facade
stage's third fold; three parsers with three semantics
become one.

- src/desc_helpers.rs: `extract_ochids` (column-0 trailer
  lines, all, in order, values trimmed) is the canonical
  parser; `extract_ochid_from_desc` becomes `.pop()` over it
  — the *last* trailer, preserving `common::extract_ochid`'s
  reverse-scan semantics on multi-ochid commits; parser
  tests moved in from squash_push plus a new multi-ochid
  last-wins test
- src/common.rs: `extract_ochid` is now a thin wrapper over
  `extract_ochid_from_desc(commit.description())`
- src/squash_push.rs: local `extract_ochids` + its three
  tests deleted; imports the desc_helpers parser
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.73.0-3
- TODO.md: ladder ref [11] filled for -2; -3 flipped to done
- notes/chores/chores-14.md: -3 As-built rung; backfill the
  0.73.0-2 `Commits:` ref [3]
```

One deliberate behavior tightening to note: the single-value accessors are now **column-0-only** — previously `common::extract_ochid` and `extract_ochid_from_desc` trimmed each line first, so an *indented* `ochid:` mention in prose would have matched; now only real trailers do (squash-push's guard already worked this way). 491 tests pass, clippy clean, installed at `0.73.0-3`. OK to push to `refactor-vc-x1`?

content.text:
Bookkeeping done. One **plan deviation to flag**: the ladder said all three copies move into `test_helpers.rs`, but `tests/cli_sync.rs` is its own test crate — it can't reach the binary's `test_helpers` module, and its variant needs `HOME` injection for config isolation. Its `jj`/`cid` moved to the crate-appropriate shared home, `tests/common/mod.rs`, with the why documented in a doc comment (and in the chores rung). The in-crate copies (push, sync) deduped onto `test_helpers` as planned.

The proposed commit:

**Title:**
```
test: jj facade fixture helpers
```

**Body:**
```
Dedup the per-module jj test helpers — the DRY-facade
stage's test-dedup step; one copy per crate.

- src/test_helpers.rs: `jj_ok` gains `cid` / `chid` /
  `description` (spawn directly, not via `crate::jj`, so
  test inspection stays independent of the facade under
  test); the doc note anticipating this migration updated
- src/push/integration_tests.rs: local `jj` / `cid` /
  `chid` / `description` deleted; call sites use the shared
  helpers; `desc_first_line` stays local over `jj_ok`
- src/sync/integration_tests.rs: local `jj` / `cid`
  deleted; call sites use the shared helpers
- tests/common/mod.rs: gains the CLI-crate `jj` (HOME +
  current_dir form) and `cid` — cli_sync's copies, moved
  here because the test crate can't reach the binary's
  test_helpers; the shape difference is documented
- tests/cli_sync.rs: helpers dropped; imports from common
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.73.0-4
- TODO.md: ladder ref [12] filled for -3; -4 flipped to done
- notes/chores/chores-14.md: -4 As-built rung (with the
  cli_sync deviation noted); backfill the 0.73.0-3
  `Commits:` ref [4]
```

491 tests pass across all 7 suites, clippy clean, installed at `0.73.0-4`. OK to push to `refactor-vc-x1`?

content.text:
Yes — stage 1 (DRY facade) is complete. All five of its bullets shipped across `-1`…`-4`: the facade module, the reinvention folds (squash_push ×3, push ×2 + inline blocks, init, sync ×3), the tracking-parser fold, the ochid-parser unification, and the test dedup.

And the 70 remaining `run(` matches are consistent with that, not evidence against it — the breakdown:

- **Trait methods, not spawns**: every subcommand's `fn run(ctx, …)` (SubcommandRunner) matches the regex — that's most of the files showing exactly 1 hit (list, desc, show, chid, config_cmd, etc., which are jj-lib in-process already).
- **Mutations, deliberately spawned**: `commit`, `describe`, `bookmark set/track`, `git push/fetch/init`, `squash`, `rebase`, `new`, `op restore` — the jj-lib migration stage's "then mutations" half, plus sync/push rollback machinery.
- **git/gh spawns in init and repo_utils** — the de-gitify-init stage's territory (and `gh` stays spawned by design).
- **`jj diff --stat` in push's review stage** — a display passthrough (raw output shown to the user), not a parsed query template.
- **Test harness spawns** — deliberately direct, per the -4 design note.

No hand-rolled `jj log -T` query template survives outside `src/jj.rs`. What's left of the cycle is the bare **0.73.0 close-out** — bookkeeping only: finalize the chores section, `## Done` entry + retire `## In Progress`, fill the `-4` ladder ref, version to bare `0.73.0`, mandatory cargo cycle. README needs no edit (no user-facing behavior changed).

One shape note for the close-out push: per your "leave main where it is," I'd push the close-out to `refactor-vc-x1` like the work commits — main stays parked at 0.71.0, and the merge-to-main moment (one trapezoid per stage, or one for the whole program — your call) comes later. Say go and I'll prepare the close-out for review.

input.description: Read ARCHITECTURE module list and README line 235 context

content.content:
- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
  exactly once. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
- `src/common.rs` — helpers the read-only commit-query
  subcommands (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) share:
  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, the
  `DotSpec` / `..`-notation parsing, `Header` /
  `resolve_header`, the `format_*` printers, and the
  `-R`/`-s` resolution stack — `find_workspace_root` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos` / `resolve_repos`
  (the top-level entry combining the `-R PATH` override and
  the `-s code|bot|code,bot` role selection).

**Subcommand-layer scaffolding:**

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`, the shared platform handle
  (today: the loaded `UserConfig` + the `--log` path; built
  once at startup via `Context::load(log)`).
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/options_flags/scope.rs` — `Scope` (a newtype over
  `Vec<Side>` — the `code`/`bot` role set) and `parse_scope`.

**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `validate_todo` / `fix_todo`
(+ `todo_helpers`), `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` and which
compose `options_flags/` leaves: `notes/chores/chores-*.md` +
`TODO.md`.

**Support:**

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model
===README235

slmkmroqtqtp 1c33ccaa567d Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)

$ vc-x1 desc -r @- -s code,bot

    Move completed items [1]-[13] and their references from todo.md to done.md.
    Replace completed jj-organization todo with new items.

    ochid: tzupykyyvnrp

=== .claude ===

# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI layer
and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer. This is the
"what + some why" — generic and slow-moving. The *how* and the
per-cycle / per-subcommand status live in `notes/chores/chores-*.md`
and `TODO.md`.

This is a living document; the tool is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with a work repo plus a `.claude` bot repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md) for the
user-facing picture and [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) for the bot
workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library embedding)
can call the same core without dragging clap along. The trigger
was `args.account.account`-style nested-leaf access leaking into
subcommand bodies; accessor shortcuts were rejected as hiding
the mismatch rather than fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**: builds `XxxParams` from the
  user-facing `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct.
  Options/flags used in more than one subcommand live as
  flattened leaves/bundles in `src/options_flags/`.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**: entrypoint shape
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>` —
  isolates the subcommand from clap and from the user.
  - `Context` — platform state shared by every subcommand.
  - `XxxParams` — that subcommand's inputs, derived from
    config / command-line args.
- **Boundary conversion**: `XxxArgs → XxxParams` via
  `impl From<&XxxArgs>` or `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`, depending
  on whether the conversion can fail.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what the
subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[the Context+Params port](#args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a TUI
   matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand returns
   a structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests
   a recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment.
The current scope is deliberately narrow: a small `Context`
(two params, not one god blob); no trait-based DI yet; one
crate, not a workspace split; `src/options_flags/` leaves left
in place — only their consumers change shape. Widen any of it
when the evidence (a second front-end, a second consumer crate,
a real platform-surface divergence) calls for it — not before.

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design used `Workspace` / `XOptions`;
the implementation (0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams`:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo
  project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand refactor status):

**CLI layer:**

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
  exactly once. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
- `src/common.rs` — helpers the read-only commit-query
  subcommands (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) share:
  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, the
  `DotSpec` / `..`-notation parsing, `Header` /
  `resolve_header`, the `format_*` printers, and the
  `-R`/`-s` resolution stack — `find_workspace_root` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos` / `resolve_repos`
  (the top-level entry combining the `-R PATH` override and
  the `-s code|bot|code,bot` role selection).

**Subcommand-layer scaffolding:**

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`, the shared platform handle
  (today: the loaded `UserConfig` + the `--log` path; built
  once at startup via `Context::load(log)`).
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/options_flags/scope.rs` — `Scope` (a newtype over
  `Vec<Side>` — the `code`/`bot` role set) and `parse_scope`.

**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `validate_todo` / `fix_todo`
(+ `todo_helpers`), `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` and which
compose `options_flags/` leaves: `notes/chores/chores-*.md` +
`TODO.md`.

**Support:**

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
   the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## args → Context + Params

"The Context+Params port" — each subcommand's
`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` is structured as
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, with an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0)
is the worked example; complete across all subcommands as of
0.49.0, and the shape new subcommands follow. Out of scope
until a real consumer surfaces: typed
errors, returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`,
`Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`. Per-subcommand
status: `notes/chores/chores-*.md` (the "Refactor tracking" section).

## per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of the Context+Params port — "the options_flags
extraction" — per-subcommand inline `#[arg]` fields are being
lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under `src/options_flags/`
so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand status.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for the options_flags extraction.
- [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) — live task list
- [`notes/chores/`](notes/chores) — chores-*.md files contain
  discussion and notes on various chores in github compatible
  markdown.

aiTitle: Implement non-ff merge and trapezoidal close-out

content.text:
Both fixes are in the working tree, riding the close-out commit:

- `src/jj.rs` added to the module inventory (with the mutations-still-spawn caveat and a pointer to the refactor program doc).
- The subcommand enumeration corrected: `finalize` dropped; `validate_bot`, `sync/state`, `squash_push`, `revert`, `config_cmd`, `bot_session` (+ `transcript`) added.

So the close-out now carries: these ARCHITECTURE.md fixes, chores finalization, the `## Done` entry + `## In Progress` retirement, the `-4` ladder ref fill, and the version bump to bare `0.73.0` — with the mandatory full cargo cycle. Say go and I'll prepare it all and present the description for review.

message.content: I think we need some TODO's for the other refactoring stages, thoughts only

content.text:
Agreed in principle — thoughts on the shape, because the naive version has a drift trap.

**Why per-stage Todos are right:** the ranked list is the working queue, and one umbrella entry hides nine remaining stages behind a single rank slot. You can't interleave — "ochid: bot-repo location qualifier" is #2, but where does *hygiene riders* rank against it? The umbrella can't say. Per-stage entries also restore the normal Preparation mechanics (a `## Todo` item to move into `## In Progress` — for 0.73.0-0 I had to synthesize the block from the stage doc instead), and each stage completion gets a natural `## Done` entry.

**The trap: two homes for the same design.** The program doc owns each stage's design and (by its own convention) each stage's status. If the Todos copy any of that, they drift. So the entries should be deliberately thin: title + a one-or-two-line gist + the anchor link into `refactor-20260716.md` — and **no status**, that stays in the doc. The existing "detail goes in notes, Todo is the pointer" convention already says exactly this.

**One nuance worth encoding: rank order ≠ dependency order.** The ranked list expresses "what next across everything"; the program doc records execution order, and some of it is real dependency, not preference — trapezoid close-out needs stateless push's no-state-growth rule; por→dual "last on purpose"; jj-lib wants split-push first. Each stage entry should carry a one-line "program stage; runs after X" so a future reprioritization can't silently violate the ordering.

**What happens to the umbrella entry (#1):** it shrinks to the program pointer — the problem statement and the stage-order line — or dissolves entirely, with each stage entry linking the doc. I lean *shrink, keep*: it's the one place the ranked list says "these N entries are one program," and it holds the shared why.

**Bloat management:** nine more entries takes the list from 13 to ~21. Two mitigations, pick either: put the far-tail stages (por→dual conversion, maybe push body-intro validation) in `todo-backlog.md` per the long-tail convention; or accept the length since the stage entries are two lines each. I'd lean backlog-for-the-tail, ranked for the rest.

Mechanically this is close-out-adjacent todo maintenance — a natural rider on the 0.73.0 close-out commit (which already retires the `## In Progress` block). content.text:
Close-out is prepared — full cargo cycle green (491 tests), installed at bare `0.73.0`. What it contains:

- **Per-stage refactor Todos** (your request): eight short ranked entries at #2–#9, each pointing at its stage anchor in `refactor-20260716.md` with a one-line prerequisite note; `por → dual conversion` to todo-backlog (#49); the umbrella entry (#1) shrunk to the program pointer with its duplicated stage-list/status bullets removed. 21 ranked entries, validates clean.
- **Cycle retirement**: `## Done` gains the DRY-facade entry (ref `[8]` → the chores-14 section); `## In Progress` back to "no cycle"; the ladder's commit refs pruned (the chores As-built ladder is the permanent record).
- **ARCHITECTURE.md**: the two fixes from your earlier prompt (jj.rs bullet; corrected subcommand enumeration).
- **chores-14**: close-out rung + a short `### Outcome` recording what the remaining `run(` surface is (mutations, de-gitify-init's git spawns, `gh` by design, the diff-stat passthrough, test harnesses) — the surface the jj-lib stage inherits.

The proposed commit:

**Title:**
```
refactor: DRY jj facade
```

**Body:**
```
Close out the DRY-facade cycle — bookkeeping only; the
facade itself landed across 0.73.0-1..-4.

- TODO.md
  - eight "Refactor stage: …" ranked entries (#2-#9), each a
    short pointer at its stage section in the program doc;
    umbrella entry #1 shrunk to the program pointer
  - `## Done` entry for the cycle; `## In Progress` retired;
    ladder commit refs pruned
- notes/todo-backlog.md: entry 49 — the por → dual
  conversion stage (last on purpose)
- ARCHITECTURE.md: `src/jj.rs` joins the module inventory;
  stale subcommand enumeration corrected (finalize dropped;
  squash_push, revert, validate_bot, config_cmd,
  bot_session/transcript, sync/state added)
- notes/chores/chores-14.md: close-out As-built rung +
  `### Outcome` (the remaining run( surface, inherited by
  the jj-lib migration stage)
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.73.0
```

**Shape at push**: per your "leave main where it is," this goes to `refactor-vc-x1` like the work commits — main stays parked; the merge-to-main trapezoid (per stage or whole program) remains open for later. OK to push?
